You are a SEALED implementer under the barrier protocol at /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/PROTOCOL.md — read it first and comply strictly.
ACCESS RULES: You must NOT read any file under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/crates/, and NOT read any Haskell source of tamarin-prover anywhere on disk (notably /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/tamarin-prover/{lib,src}/ and /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/tamarin-prover-testing/{lib,src}/). The ONLY Haskell you may read is the SANCTIONED BSD library /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/graphdot/sanctioned/pretty-1.1.3.6/. Do NOT read /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/INTEGRATION_REPORT.md or TAIL_ELIMINATION_SCOPE.md, and do NOT read any AUDIT.md anywhere in the cleanroom. No web access (the local HS oracle server is not web access). You MAY read .spthy examples under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/tamarin-prover/examples/, the flags map /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/scripts/file_flags.tsv, and the captured web corpus /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/scripts/.web_hs_cache. No git commits.
DISCIPLINE: log every oracle interaction in the pretty workspace QUERIES.log; record behavioral facts in BEHAVIOR.md (new "Web mode (R6)" sections) with provenance — write findings DURABLY AS YOU GO (this run may be interrupted and resumed; a successor continues from your workspace state). FIRST MOVE on any start: check BEHAVIOR.md/QUERIES.log for existing R6 web-mode sections and partial src work, and CONTINUE incrementally rather than restarting. Oracle/server scripts carry OOM guards — preserve them; live HS servers only on ports 3100-3199; STOP every server you start before returning. Return a PLAIN-TEXT summary.

Workspaces: /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/pretty/ (your primary crate workspace/pretty-clean, R1-R5 done: full batch echo at 110/73 plain text) and READ access to /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/weblayer/ sealed materials (except AUDIT.md) — especially producers/workspace/producers-clean (the pane SKIN: escape_text, postprocess_lines, envelopes — already sealed and byte-proven) and its BEHAVIOR.md, plus the live oracle weblayer/producers/oracle/hs_server.sh and capture tools weblayer/producers/oracle/extract_fragments.py.

THE TASK — round 6: the WEB RENDERING MODE of theory content. The interactive server renders the same theory blocks your crate already models, but differently: HTML-escaped, wrapped in hl_* highlight spans, laid out at width 100 (not 110/73), WITHOUT the batch header comment. This mode fills the main/message and main/rules pane BODIES (the producers cluster modeled those bodies as OPAQUE input — your job is to produce them) and the source views. Corpus: the pane bodies across the 81 captured manifests (message: signature/construction/deconstruction sections; rules: macros/fact-symbols/MSR/restrictions sections); live probes for anything the corpus does not pin.
SCOPE, priority ordered by which ported callers it retires:
1. CHARACTERIZE the mode from captures first: the hl_* span CLASS VOCABULARY over theory tokens (census every distinct class + what token kinds carry it), span boundary rules (does a span cover the keyword only? operator glyphs? fact names? comments?), the escape set interaction (you may reuse producers escape_text — verify identical), the layout width and ribbon at web mode (probe wrap positions: find lines that wrap in panes but not in the batch echo and vice versa; pin the exact width/ribbon pair), and any content differences vs the batch echo (no header comment; section headers; what separates rules).
2. IMPLEMENT a parameterized web-mode render path in pretty-clean: your Doc engine already renders at arbitrary (width, ribbon) — add the HTML emission (escape + span injection at the pinned boundaries) as a second render target over your EXISTING block renderers (rules, signature items, restrictions, macros, predicates, lemma/formula text as it appears in source views). Do NOT duplicate the block logic — one model, two render targets.
3. ACCEPTANCE: a corpus sweep asserting pane-BODY byte-identity — compose your web-mode output through the producers-clean skin (postprocess_lines + envelope) and byte-compare against ALL captured main/message and main/rules bodies across the 81 manifests (the producers R1 sweep methodology — their tests show how); plus live replays on at least 2 never-captured theories; plus a mutation check. Record UNOBSERVABLE corners honestly.
All cargo tests green (R1-R5 suites untouched); zero warnings. Do NOT edit anything outside /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/pretty/ (weblayer is read-only for you).

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- artifact-capabilities: Runtime capabilities a published Artifact can declare — calling the user's claude.ai connectors (MCP) from the page, and future abilities. Load this BEFORE passing `capabilities` to the Artifact tool or writing any `window.claude.mcp` code.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# Clean-room reimplementation protocol — tamarin-rs relicensing
2	
3	Purpose: independently recreate three modules of tamarin-rs (wellformedness
4	checker, graph/dot pipeline, web layer) without access to GPL-covered
5	expression, so the results are original works relicensable at will.
6	
7	## Rooms
8	- DIRTY ROOM (Kevin Milner + main-session assistant): has read the GPL Haskell
9	  source and the ported Rust modules. Produces only: interoperability headers
10	  (type surfaces, entry-point signatures, comments stripped), oracle wrappers,
11	  and behavioral bug reports. Never contributes implementation text.
12	- CLEAN ROOM (isolated agents, one per cluster): receive ONLY the materials
13	  listed per cluster below. Their complete tool-call transcripts are retained
14	  as evidence of non-access.
15	
16	## Clean-room materials (exhaustive)
17	1. This protocol, the cluster SPEC.md, and interface/ headers (type surfaces +
18	   boundary signatures only).
19	2. Black-box oracles: `wf_oracle.sh` (runs the compiled Haskell binary on an
20	   input file), `hs_server.sh` (serves a theory in the Haskell web UI for HTTP
21	   probing), pre-captured oracle outputs (oracle/captures/,
22	   oracle/captured_responses/), and GPL example .spthy files used solely as
23	   observation INPUTS.
24	3. Public Rust toolchain and general documentation.
25	
26	## Forbidden to the clean room
27	- Reading ANY file under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/ — source, comments, git
28	  history — with the sole exceptions of executing the oracle scripts and
29	  reading oracle/captured_responses/ (captured program OUTPUT, not source).
30	- Fetching tamarin-prover source, docs describing its internals, or its GitHub.
31	- Reproducing any tamarin-prover implementation detail from model memory:
32	  every behavior must be derived from the oracle, the captures, or the SPEC.
33	  Exact output strings (warning texts, DOT syntax, HTML) MUST be taken from
34	  observed oracle output — that is compatibility content, not expression.
35	
36	## Audit & integration (after implementation)
37	1. Transcript audit: verify from the retained agent transcripts that no
38	   forbidden path was accessed.
39	2. Similarity audit: a reviewer (may see both sides) compares the clean code
40	   against the Haskell originals; flagged similarities are sent back as
41	   redo-instructions, never patched directly.
42	3. Behavioral acceptance: the FULL-CORPUS gate for the slice must pass, not a
43	   fixture subset (see "Full-corpus gates" below). A narrow fixture suite that
44	   passes while the corpus gate fails is not acceptance.
45	4. Integration: the open side may write thin adapters and mechanical renames to
46	   fit the workspace API, but must not transplant logic from the replaced
47	   modules. The replaced modules are deleted; their GPL-pending headers go
48	   with them (history retains them under GPL). Every integrator that touches a
49	   slice with a full-corpus gate MUST run that gate before declaring green.
50	
51	## Full-corpus gates (standard, not optional)
52	
53	Each slice has a fast oracle gate over the whole 419-file example corpus,
54	diffing the slice's output against the Haskell reference cache. These are the
55	sealed implementer's primary data source (run between build and the HS cache
56	every iteration) AND the integrator's mandatory acceptance check. Both take
57	`RS_PATH` (point at any build) and `ALLOWLIST` (subset while iterating).
58	
59	- Wellformedness: `scripts/wf_gate.sh` — runs the binary WITHOUT `--prove`
60	  (the wf block prints at theory-load, ~1s/file; full corpus ~72s at JOBS=6),
61	  extracts the `/* WARNING … */` block, diffs vs `scripts/.hs_file_cache`.
62	- Web: `scripts/web_parity.sh` — crawls HS and RS interactive servers per
63	  theory, semantic-diffs the response manifests vs `scripts/.web_hs_cache`.
64	- Full batch (proof output, slow ~30-60min): `scripts/corpus_file_diff.sh` —
65	  the ground-truth `--prove` byte diff; run at milestones, not every iteration.
66	
67	Once a slice's ported code is DELETED, the integrated module IS the sealed
68	reimplementation, so running the gate against the tree observes the sealed
69	code's own output vs the reference — legitimate feedback, not observation of
70	replaced code. For a not-yet-integrated slice the tree still holds the ported
71	code, so gate only the workspace build (via `RS_PATH`), never the stock tree.
72

content.content:
1	# BEHAVIOR.md — inferred behavioral spec of the theory echo (R1: term core + signature block; R2: rule blocks; R3: restriction/lemma formulas)
2	
3	Every claim traces to a logged oracle probe (QUERIES.log) or a pre-materialized
4	round-1 capture (`round1/targets/*.hs.txt` — itself oracle output). Notation:
5	"target:<file>" = observed in that capture; "probe:<name>" = constructed
6	.spthy run through `oracle/pretty_oracle.sh` (files under
7	workspace/scratchpad/probes/, outputs kept beside them as `.out`).
8	
9	## Layout engine
10	
11	HughesPJ Doc at line width 110, ribbon 73 (SPEC-provided parameters; all wrap
12	observations below are reproduced by the sanctioned BSD engine at these
13	settings). Key engine consequences confirmed against probes:
14	
15	* A one-line candidate is kept iff it fits within ribbon 73 measured from the
16	  current line position; an exactly-73-column line still fits
17	  (probe:e_mid — 73-column `equations:` one-liner kept; probe:b_all —
18	  builtins item that would end at column 75 wraps).
19	* Fill continuations align at the fill's origin column, and that indent does
20	  NOT consume ribbon: continuation content is measured from the alignment
21	  column (probe:b_all functions line 2 reaches display column 79 = 11 + 68;
22	  probe:t_wide App continuation reaches display column 78).
23	* Nested content (e.g. rule bodies at indent 3) likewise measures its ribbon
24	  from the nest, so lines up to 76 display columns appear at nest 3
25	  (probe:t_edge).
26	
27	* **Deep-structure robustness (R4 blocker 3, BYTE-NEUTRAL).** A rendered Doc is
28	  a per-LINE linked chain (`NilAbove`/`TextBeside`/`Nest` interleaved with
29	  memoised `Lazy(Forced)` thunks) as deep as the theory has lines. A huge
30	  `variants (modulo AC)` block reaches ~10 000 lines (C8's
31	  `Terminal_Receives_Records_LocalAuth_C8` is 9 886 lines; BP_IBS_2/3/4 ~5–6k),
32	  so the naive recursive `reduceDoc`/`reduceVert`/`reduceHoriz` (build), the
33	  display walk (`lay`), and — the last one to surface — the compiler-generated
34	  recursive `Drop` of the nested `Rc<Doc>` all overflow a normal 8 MB stack
35	  (measured: overflow between ~1 500 and ~2 000 two-line groups). The wide FILL
36	  machinery is already lazy and does not overflow; only these eager `Above`/
37	  spine/drop walks do. All four are rewritten to iterate over a heap `Vec`
38	  (spine unrolled then folded; `lay` a loop; an explicit-stack `Drop` for
39	  `Doc`) — a pure evaluation-order change, so every rendered byte is identical
40	  (whole existing R1/R2/R3 suites unchanged) and the four deep witness files
41	  render completely and byte-match the oracle. Proof: a 12 000-group block
42	  renders on a 2 MB stack, and the round-2 curated set (now including the four
43	  deep files) passes on a production 8 MB stack, not the former 512 MB one.
44	
45	## Signature section
46	
47	Overall shape (all targets/probes):
48	
49	```
50	// Function signature and definition of the equational theory E
51	<blank>
52	builtins: …        (line ABSENT when no surviving entries)
53	functions: …       (always present — base symbols guarantee items)
54	equations: …       (always present — base equations)
55	```
56	
57	The three declaration lines are adjacent (no blank lines between them).
58	
59	### `builtins:` line
60	
61	* Only these builtins keep a builtins-line entry: `diffie-hellman`,
62	  `bilinear-pairing`, `multiset`, `natural-numbers`, `xor`
63	  (probes:b_<name>). All others (hashing, signing, revealing-signing,
64	  symmetric-encryption, asymmetric-encryption, dest-*, locations-report)
65	  vanish from the line, contributing functions/equations instead
66	  (probes:b_*, b_locrep; target:classic_NSLPK3 has no builtins line).
67	* `bilinear-pairing` INDUCES `diffie-hellman` (probe:b_bilinear-pairing:
68	  echo `builtins: diffie-hellman, bilinear-pairing`; target:sp14_Joux).
69	* Canonical order regardless of source order: diffie-hellman <
70	  bilinear-pairing < multiset < natural-numbers < xor (probe:b_all — source
71	  order was scrambled; target:features_multiset_NumberSubtermTests).
72	* Duplicates collapse (probe:b_dupline).
73	* Layout: `text "builtins: " <> fsep (punctuate ',' names)` — items
74	  comma-separated, single space, fill-wrapped, continuation aligned at
75	  column 10 (after `builtins: `), comma attached to the preceding item
76	  (probe:b_all, target:features_multiset_NumberSubtermTests).
77	
78	### `functions:` line
79	
80	* Items render `name/arity` + attribute suffix, comma-separated.
81	* Attribute suffix (probe:f_attrs, probe:b_locrep, probes:b_dest-*):
82	  - public constructor → no suffix (`c/3`)
83	  - private constructor → `[private,constructor]`
84	  - public destructor → `[destructor]`
85	  - private destructor → `[private,destructor]`
86	  No space before `[`; no spaces inside.
87	* Sort: ASCII byte order on the name, case-sensitive (`Bb/1, Zz/1, a1/2, aa/1,
88	  cA/0` — probe:f_sort).
89	* Dedup: identical (name,arity) between user decls and builtin expansions, or
90	  repeated user decls, appear once (probe:f_dedup). Conflicting arity or
91	  attribute redeclarations are REJECTED upstream by the tool
92	  (probe:f_dedup2 raw error), so the renderer never sees them.
93	* Typed declarations (`kdf(bitstring):skey`) erase to `kdf/1`
94	  (probe:f_typed).
95	* Base symbols always present: `fst/1, pair/2, snd/1` (probe:b_none);
96	  with `dest-pairing` instead: `fst/1[destructor], pair/2, snd/1[destructor]`
97	  (probe:b_dest-pairing).
98	* Per-builtin function expansions (compatibility content, from probes
99	  b_hashing / b_asymmetric-encryption / b_signing / b_symmetric-encryption /
100	  b_revealing-signing / b_locrep / b_dest-*):
101	  - hashing → `h/1`
102	  - asymmetric-encryption → `adec/2, aenc/2, pk/1`
103	    (dest-asymmetric-encryption: `adec/2[destructor]`)
104	  - signing → `pk/1, sign/2, true/0, verify/3`
105	    (dest-signing: `verify/3[destructor]`)
106	  - symmetric-encryption → `sdec/2, senc/2`
107	    (dest-symmetric-encryption: `sdec/2[destructor]`)
108	  - revealing-signing → `getMessage/1, pk/1, revealSign/2, revealVerify/3,
109	    true/0`
110	  - locations-report → `check_rep/2[destructor], get_rep/1[destructor],
111	    rep/2[private,constructor], report/1`
112	  - diffie-hellman / bilinear-pairing / multiset / natural-numbers / xor →
113	    none (their operators/constants — one, inv, DH_neutral, zero, ++, %+ —
114	    never appear in `functions:`; probes b_diffie-hellman, b_xor, …).
115	* Layout: `text "functions: " <> fsep (punctuate ',' items)` — fill wrap,
116	  continuation aligned at column 11 (probe:b_revealing-signing, probe:b_all,
117	  probe:e_long).

119	### `equations:` block
120	
121	* Header `equations:`, or `equations [convergent]:` when the user block is
122	  declared `[convergent]` (probe:e_conv).
123	* Base equations always present: `fst(<x.1, x.2>) = x.1`,
124	  `snd(<x.1, x.2>) = x.2` (probe:b_none).
125	* Per-builtin equation expansions (probes as above):
126	  - asymmetric-encryption → `adec(aenc(x.1, pk(x.2)), x.2) = x.1`
127	  - signing → `verify(sign(x.1, x.2), x.1, pk(x.2)) = true`
128	  - symmetric-encryption → `sdec(senc(x.1, x.2), x.2) = x.1`
129	  - revealing-signing → `getMessage(revealSign(x.1, x.2)) = x.1`,
130	    `revealVerify(revealSign(x.1, x.2), x.1, pk(x.2)) = true`
131	  - locations-report → `check_rep(rep(x.1, x.2), x.2) = x.1`,
132	    `get_rep(rep(x.1, x.2)) = x.1`
133	  - hashing → none
134	* Sort: STRUCTURAL order on the (lhs, rhs) pair — NOT byte order of the
135	  rendered text (refuted by target:contract — `checkpcs(xc, xpk, …)` prints
136	  before `checkpcs(xc, pk(xsk), …)` though 'x' > 'p' — and by target:mesh's
137	  get_b1/get_b2/aes_cmac groups) and NOT declaration order in either
138	  direction (probes p_eqA/p_eqB and p_eqF/p_eqF2: reversed sources echo
139	  identically; contract/mesh source order is the reverse of the echo). The
140	  pinned comparison, lexicographic (lhs first, rhs breaks lhs ties —
141	  probes p_eqF/p_eqF2):
142	  - variables sort BELOW all applications regardless of names
143	    (probes p_eqC/p_eqC2: `f(zzz, …)` < `f(a0, …)` with a0/0 nullary;
144	    corpus witnesses contract `xpk` < `pk(xsk)`, mesh `cnf` <
145	    `aes_cmac(…)`);
146	  - variable vs variable: name bytes (NOT shortlex — probe:p_eqD `azz` <
147	    `b`), then index (probe:p_eqH user `x` < builtin `x.1`);
148	  - application vs application: head name bytes FIRST, then arguments
149	    left-to-right (contract checkpcs decided at argument 2; head-name byte
150	    order across groups: mesh `aes_ccm_dec` < `aes_cmac`, refuting
151	    shortlex); arity never discriminates observably except via `pair`
152	    (probe:p_eqG refutes arity-first: `pair`/2 < `z1`/1);
153	  - tuples compare as RIGHT-NESTED binary applications named `pair`
154	    (probe:p_eqE `g(…)` < `<x, y>`; probe:p_eqG `<x, y>` < `z1(…)`;
155	    probe:p_eqI `<x, zz>` < `<x, b, c>` — the binary view compares var `zz`
156	    vs app `pair(b, c)`; a flattened elementwise view would reverse them).
157	  UNOBSERVABLE ranks (cannot occur in an equations block, flagged): literal
158	  constants (rejected upstream — probe:e_samehead raw), exp/AC operators,
159	  diff, pattern-match. Implemented as application-rank under their rendered
160	  head spelling; corpus gate is the backstop if they ever occur.
161	* Dedup: EXACT duplicates collapse (probe:e_dup); alpha-equivalent but
162	  differently-named equations do NOT (probe:e_adedup keeps user
163	  `fst(<a, b>) = a` alongside the builtin fst equation).
164	* Layout: `sep (header : map (nest 4) (punctuate ',' eqDocs))` — i.e.
165	  all-or-nothing, NOT fill:
166	  - fits on one line (≤ 73) → `equations: e1, e2, e3` (probe:e_mid at
167	    exactly 73);
168	  - otherwise header alone, then EVERY equation on its own line at indent 4
169	    with trailing comma on all but the last — even when several would fit
170	    joined at indent 4 (probe:e_conv: joined length 71 still one-per-line).
171	* Each equation is `sep [lhsDoc, nest (-2) (text "= " <> rhsDoc)]`:
172	  one line `lhs = rhs`; when too long, `= rhs` drops to (equation indent − 2)
173	  (probe:e_long — `  = xlongvariablename1` at column 2 under equations at
174	  indent 4).

176	## Term rendering
177	
178	Observed atoms:
179	
180	| AST shape | rendered | provenance |
181	|---|---|---|
182	| var, untagged/msg | `x` | everywhere |
183	| var, fresh | `~x` | target:cav13 (`x:fresh` source → `~x`) |
184	| var, pub | `$A` | target:cav13 |
185	| var, node | `#i` | targets (lemma text) |
186	| var, nat | `%x` | target:NumberSubtermTests |
187	| var with index k>0 | `name.k` after the sigil (`~x.7`, `x.10`, `XB.10`) | targets: variants + builtin eqs |
188	| pub literal | `'g'`, `'hello_world'` | probe:t_pair |
189	| fresh literal | `~'n'` | probe:t_frlit |
190	| nat literal 1 (`%1`, `1:nat`) | `%1` | probe:t_nat |
191	| nat literal n (`2:nat`) | `%2` | probe:t_num2 |
192	| DH one (`1` in DH theory) | `one` | probe:t_one, probe:t_gone |
193	| DH neutral | `DH_neutral` | probe:t_gone, target:cav13 variant 7 |
194	| xor zero | `zero` (nullary-app form) | probe:t_xor |
195	
196	Composite shapes:
197	
198	* Application: `f(a1, a2)` — `text f <> "(" <> fsep (punctuate ',' args) <>
199	  ")"`; commas attach to the preceding arg, fill space between args, wrap
200	  aligns after the `(`, closing `)` attached to the last arg
201	  (probe:t_wide W2 — continuation line `ylongvariablename2,
202	  zlongvariablename3)`; probe:e_long). Nullary symbols render bare (`shk`,
203	  `zero`, `true`, `f` — targets + probes).
204	  The application `)` is the plain BESIDE `<> ")"`, NOT a droppable fill item:
205	  when the last argument is a multi-line tuple whose `>` drops to its own line,
206	  the `)` STAYS JOINED to it as `>)` (probe:appdrop `someop(…, <…, macf(…)>)`
207	  → `…dd>)`; probe:appdrop2 nested → `…>))`; the round-2 blocker-2 corpus —
208	  ake/dh UM_three_pass etc. — has MAC applications with this exact shape). This
209	  is the crux of R4 blocker 2: the enclosing-delimiter drop is operator-
210	  specific — an AC-operator/pair `)`/`>` DROPS (fill item), an application `)`
211	  does NOT (beside). Changing `app_doc` to drop the `)` would break these
212	  witnesses; the fix is to leave it beside.
213	* Pair: `<a, b, c>` — ONE fill (`fcat`) whose items are `<`, each element
214	  with its attached `", "` under `nest 1`, and `>`:
215	  `fcat ('<' : map (nest 1) (punctuate ", " elems) ++ ['>'])`. This single
216	  construction reproduces every observed shape (probe:p_pw1 wfa–wfd,
217	  target:mesh k2 equation, probe:t_wide W1):
218	  - fits → `<a, b>` on one line; a wrapped fill line ends with the trailing
219	    space of the attached `", "` (probe:t_wide W1 byte-checked);
220	  - overflow elements continue at (column of `<`) + 1 — the nest 1;
221	  - when the FIRST element's one-liner does not fit beside `<`, the `<`
222	    stays ALONE on its line (probe:p_pw1 wfb; mesh k2 inner tuple);
223	  - `>` sits beside the last element when that element ends a fill line
224	    (p_pw1 wfa/wfb/wfd), but drops to its own line at the column of `<`
225	    (no nest) when the last element is multi-line (p_pw1 wfc; mesh k2
226	    outer tuple) — the fill places items after a multi-line item below it.
227	  The earlier R1 law (`"<" <> fcat … <> ">"`, `<`/`>` always attached) was
228	  WRONG — falsified by mesh k2 / p_pw1; it agreed with the true law only on
229	  the shapes R1 observed (fits, and wfa/wfd-style wraps).
230	  Right-nested pairs flatten (`<x, <y, z>>` → `<x, y, z>`);
231	  a pair in NON-last position stays delimited: `<<x, y>, z>` (probe:t_pair).
232	  Source `pair(x, y)` arrives already normalized to a pair (probe:t_pair).
233	* Exponentiation: `a^b`, chains render FLAT for both left- and right-nested
234	  trees: `('g'^~x)^~y` AND `'g'^(~x^~y)` both echo `'g'^~x^~y`
235	  (probe:t_exp2). No parentheses are ever added around exp or its operands
236	  (operand classes that need delimiting — AC ops — are self-parenthesizing).
237	* AC operators mult `*`, xor `⊕` (U+2295), multiset `++`, nat-plus `%+`:
238	  always wrapped in parens, no spaces, arguments flattened across BOTH sides
239	  regardless of source nesting: `(x⊕y⊕z)`, `(a++b++c)`, `(%x%+%y%+%z)`,
240	  `('g'-exponent (~x*~y*~z))` (probes:t_xor, t_uni, t_nat, t_mult2, t_exp2 —
241	  the AC rule variants normalize (g^x)^y to `'g'^(~x*~y)`).
242	  The parens are intrinsic: they appear in every context (function argument
243	  `inv((~x.7*x.11))` — target:cav13 variants; fact argument `Out( (~x⊕~y) )`).
244	  Layout on overflow: the SAME single-fill construction as tuples, with the
245	  delimiters as fill items —
246	  `fcat ('(' : map (nest 1) (punctuate op elems) ++ [')'])`:
247	  break between elements with the operator attached to the preceding element
248	  and no fill space, continuation at (column of `(`) + 1 (probe:t_uniwide:
249	  line ends `…aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa3++`), and the `)` drops to its OWN line at
250	  the column of `(` when it does not fit beside the last element
251	  (round-3 target:alethea Universal_VerProofV_v1 — the union keeps both wide
252	  tuple elements on one 71-column fill line and only `)))"` drops). The
253	  earlier R1 law (`"(" <> fcat … <> ")"`, `)` always attached) agreed with
254	  the true construction on every R1-observed shape but was falsified by the
255	  alethea witness.
256	  The R4 blocker-2 corpus (ake/dh: UM_three_pass{,_combined,_combined_fixed},
257	  DHKEA{,_keyreg}) exercises the OTHER `)`-drop sub-case: the LAST union element
258	  is itself a multi-line tuple, so the tuple's `>` drops to its `<` column AND
259	  the union's `)` drops BELOW it to the `(` column, on SEPARATE lines
260	  (probe:uniondrop `(<'1', …>++<'2', dd, macf(…)>)` →
261	  `…>` / `…)` on their own lines; UM_three_pass echo lines ~54–55, whole-block
262	  byte-parity re-asserted). The alethea round-3 fix (`)` as a fill item)
263	  already covers this; the round-2 curated set now re-asserts it across the
264	  five ake/dh files. (Contrast the application `)` above, which stays `>)`.)
265	* diff: `diff(x, y)` — application form (probe:t_diff, run with --diff).
266	* mult inside exp exponent: `x.10^(x.11*inv(x.12))` (target:cav13) — normal
267	  AC-paren rule, no extra exp parens.
268	
269	## Rule blocks (R2)
270	
271	Curated byte targets: round2/targets/*.hs.txt (12 corpus files, 72 rule
272	blocks, every block byte-verified by tests/round2_rules.rs round-trip
273	parity). A rule BLOCK is:
274	
275	```
276	rule (modulo E) Name[attrs]:
277	   <body>
278	<blank line>
279	  // loop breaker(s): [..]        (optional, col 2)
280	  <variants comment>              (col 2)
281	```
282	
283	### Header
284	
285	* `rule (modulo E) Name:` — the modulo annotation is input data (`E` on the
286	  closed rule, `AC` on the variant re-render inside the comment).
287	* Attribute list attaches to the name: `Name[color=#abcdef, process="…",
288	  no_derivcheck, issapicrule, role='r']:` — canonical order color < process <
289	  no_derivcheck < issapicrule < role regardless of source order; the LAST
290	  color/process/role declaration wins; external `x-…` attributes are DROPPED
291	  (probe:p_rattr, target:issue713). Spellings: `color=#<hex>` unquoted
292	  (lowercased upstream: '#AbCdEf' → `#abcdef`), `role='…'` single-quoted.
293	  Fill-wrap aligned after the `[`, `]:`  attached to the last item
294	  (probe:p_rattr R3, target:issue713 bla).
295	* **SAPIC `process` attribute** (R4 blocker-1 correction; probe:p_process,
296	  target:ct — the earlier "process is DROPPED" law was WRONG, from a corpus
297	  that had no `issapicrule` files in the round-2 set). It is present on every
298	  SAPIC-generated (`issapicrule`) rule, ABSENT otherwise, and renders
299	  `process="<snippet>"` — DOUBLE quotes (contrast `role='…'`), positioned
300	  between `color` and `no_derivcheck`. The `<snippet>` is the translated
301	  process step, carried VERBATIM as one unbreakable text token: `process="|"`
302	  (parallel), `process="!"` (replication), `process="0"` (null),
303	  `process="in(x.1);"`, `process="event Ev( x.1 );"`,
304	  `process="out(<x.1, 'lbl'>);"`, `process="lookup <'att', h.3> as a.1"`,
305	  `process="insert <$ca.2, 'proofOfID', $s.1, pk(skS.1)>,'yes';"`. Its interior
306	  spaces, commas, `<>`, `()`, `;` and SINGLE-quoted string constants are part
307	  of the value, NOT layout — the fill never breaks inside it (target:ct 100+
308	  witnesses; probe:p_process). ESCAPING of `"`/`\` is UNOBSERVABLE: process
309	  snippets quote their constants with `'…'`, so no `"`/`\`/control char ever
310	  reaches the attribute; rendered with no escaping (flagged — a corpus with a
311	  process containing `"`/`\` would be needed to pin it).
312	
313	### Body
314	
315	* `nest 3 (sep [prem-group, nest (-1) arrow-group, concl-group])` — one line
316	  `[ … ] --[ … ]-> [ … ]` when it fits, otherwise three rows: groups at
317	  col 3, arrow at col 2. TRUE sep, not a fill: cav13 Step1 keeps `-->` on
318	  its own row although it would fit beside the premise row.
319	* Bracket groups (premises, conclusions, AND the action arrow — one
320	  construction, probe:p_arr1): `sep [sep [open, fsep facts], close]`:
321	  - one line `[ f1, f2 ]` when the whole group fits (kept at exactly
322	    ribbon 73 — probe:p_arr1 PR);
323	  - else `close` drops to the group's column; `open` keeps ALL facts beside
324	    it iff they fit that line as a unit (target:mesh DeviceWaitingUser at
325	    exactly 73 vs ProvisionerWaitingUser one column over);
326	  - else `open` is alone too, facts fill-wrap at the group's column
327	    (target:NSLPK3 R_1 three facts per fill line; target:Tutorial Serv_1 —
328	    `--[` alone although the first action alone would fit beside it).
329	  Empty list → `[ ]`; empty actions → the literal `-->`.
330	* Fact: `sep [name<>"(" <+> fsep args, ")"]` — `Name( a, b )`, args
331	  fill-wrap aligned after `Name( `, `)` drops alone to the fact's column
332	  when the args are multi-line (probe:t_wide); `Name( )` when nullary
333	  (target:mesh); `!` prefix on persistent facts (target:NSLPK3).
334	* Fact annotations attach directly after the `)`: `[+, -, no_precomp]` —
335	  canonical order + < - < no_precomp regardless of source order
336	  (probe:p_fann `[no_precomp,+]` → `[+, no_precomp]`;
337	  target:seqdfsneeded `[no_precomp,-]` → `[-, no_precomp]`).
338	
339	### Loop breakers
340	
341	* `// loop breaker: [0]` singular / `// loop breakers: [0,1]` plural,
342	  0-based premise indices, comma WITHOUT space (probes c_loop, p_lb2).
343	* E-rule side: on its own line at col 2, between the blank line and the
344	  variants comment. AC-rule side: INSIDE the comment at col 4, AFTER the
345	  variants list (probe:p_lbvar).
346	
347	### The variants comment
348	
349	* Trivial: `/* has exactly the trivial AC variant */` at col 2.
350	* Otherwise (col 2 `/*` … `*/`):
351	  ```
352	  /*
353	  rule (modulo AC) Name:
354	     <body, same layout, nested +2>
355	    variants (modulo AC)          (col 4; ABSENT when no substitutions —
356	    <numbered groups>              target:issue777 macro-expanded AC rule)
357	    // loop breaker: [..]         (optional, col 4)
358	  */
359	  ```
360	* Numbered groups at col 4: the index is RIGHT-ALIGNED to the widest
361	  `N.` (`1.` cav13/7 variants; ` 9.`/`12.` CH07; `  1.`/`160.` Joux),
362	  followed by one space; the group's substitution lines align after that
363	  prefix. Groups are separated by a line of BARE INDENT (four spaces at
364	  this nesting — trailing whitespace, byte-checked probe:p_var1); no
365	  separator after the last group.
366	* One substitution = `lhs $$ nest 6 ("= " <> rhs)` with HughesPJ overlap:
367	  a lhs narrower than 6 columns is padded to column 6 (`~lv2  = ~lv2.4`,
368	  `~ltkS = …` at exactly 5+1); a lhs of ≥ 6 columns pushes `= rhs` to its
369	  own line at column 6 relative to the entry (probe:p_var1
370	  `~longvariablenameone`, target:Tutorial `request`). The rhs is a full R1
371	  term with its own wrapping (target:mesh multi-line aes_cmac values).
372	  The lhs/rhs substitution DATA is solver-provided input; both sides render
373	  through the R1 term core.
374	
375	### `macros:` block (rule-adjacent surface)
376	
377	* `text "macros: " <> vcat (punctuate ',' items)` — the block ALWAYS breaks
378	  (R5 GAP-2 correction; the earlier `sep`/all-or-nothing law was WRONG). The
379	  first macro sits beside `macros: ` and every subsequent macro goes on its
380	  own line aligned after `macros: ` (col 8), REGARDLESS of fit
381	  (probe:r5_mac2 — two short macros that would fit one line still break;
382	  probe:p_mac1; target:issue777 / probe:r5_mac1 — a lone macro is trivially
383	  one line). Commas attach to the preceding macro; the last carries none.
384	  (`sep` agreed with the true `vcat` law only when the list overflowed, which
385	  every earlier witness happened to do — probe:r5_mac2 is the first
386	  fits-but-still-breaks witness.)
387	* Item: fact-style head with the `)` ATTACHED to the last param line
388	  (unlike facts), then `) =  body` — two spaces after `=` (the `= ` token
389	  plus hsep spacing); the body always sits beside and wraps internally
390	  (probe:p_mac1 wide macro).
391	
392	### Out of R2 scope (recorded, not implemented here)
393	
394	* The theory-level `/* looping facts with injective instances: A/1 */`
395	  comment (probe:c_loop) sits between the signature and the rules — top
396	  level assembly, not the rule block.
397	* Embedded rule restrictions (`_restrict`) never surfaced in any probed
398	  echo (TPM_DAA corpus files lift them to top-level restrictions upstream);
399	  no rendering law claimed.
400	* Diff-mode (`--diff`) rule surfaces (left/right variants) are not covered
401	  by the round-2 curated set; a later round must pin them before diff-file
402	  parity is claimed.

404	## Formula rendering (R3)
405	
406	Curated byte targets: round3/targets/*.hs.txt (20 corpus files; 84
407	restriction + 139 lemma blocks, every block byte-verified by
408	tests/round3_formulas.rs round-trip parity).
409	
410	### Glyphs and atoms
411	
412	| construct | rendered | provenance |
413	|---|---|---|
414	| true / false | `⊤` / `⊥` | probe:q_w1 |
415	| conjunction / disjunction | `∧` / `∨` | probe:q_at1, q_p2 |
416	| implication / iff | `⇒` / `⇔` | probe:q_p2, q_r2 |
417	| negation | `¬(…)` — argument ALWAYS parenthesized | probe:q_p2 s8 |
418	| quantifiers | `∀` / `∃` | everywhere |
419	| action atom | `Fact( … ) @ tp` — spaces around `@` | probe:q_at1 |
420	| temporal/nat order | `t1 < t2` | probe:q_at1 |
421	| equality | `t1 = t2` | probe:q_at1 |
422	| subterm | `t1 ⊏ t2` (source `<<` and `⊏` both echo `⊏`) | probe:q_at1, target:NumberSubtermTests |
423	| last | `last(tp)` — NO interior spaces (unlike facts) | probe:q_at1 |
424	
425	Terms inside atoms render through the R1 term core unchanged (probe:q_l4 —
426	pair trailing-`", "` wrap, AC self-parens); action facts through the R2 fact
427	construction unchanged, `!` prefix included (target:NSLPK3 `!KU( ni ) @ #j`,
428	probe:q_l3).
429	
430	### Parenthesization
431	
432	* EVERY operand of a binary connective (∧ ∨ ⇒ ⇔) is wrapped in `(…)`,
433	  whatever it is — atom, ⊤/⊥, ¬, quantifier, another connective
434	  (probe:q_p2 s1–s13; targets NSLPK3/Cronto/acc).
435	* `¬`'s argument is always wrapped (double wrap when ¬ is itself an
436	  operand: `(¬(x = 'd'))`).
437	* Quantifier BODIES and the top level are bare (probe:q_p2, q_w1).
438	* Chains render their source association faithfully — `a | b | c` parses
439	  left-nested upstream and echoes `((a) ∨ (b)) ∨ (c)`; no flattening,
440	  no re-association (probe:q_at1, probe:q_p2 s3–s6).
441	
442	### Layout
443	
444	* Binary connective = `sep [lhs-operand <+> glyph, rhs-operand]`: one line
445	  `(A) ∧ (B)` when it fits; otherwise the glyph stays on the lhs' LAST line
446	  and the rhs drops to the group origin (targets NSLPK3 types /
447	  Yubikey slightly_weaker_invariant / Cronto notSameRole deep left chain).
448	* Quantifier = `sep [glyph<>" "<>fsep binders<>".", nest 1 body]`:
449	  - binders are space-separated in source order with their R1 sigils and
450	    `.idx` suffixes (`∀ x.1 a.1 #i.1.` — probe:q_b1, target:acc lemmas);
451	  - the binder fill wraps aligned after `"∀ "` (origin+2 — probe:q_l2 bw1);
452	  - the `.` attaches to the last binder;
453	  - the body sits beside after one space, or drops to (quantifier origin+1)
454	    — the nest 1 (probe:q_l2 bw1/bw2; paren-nested `(((∃ #j.` → body at
455	    paren col + 2 confirms the same nest at depth).
456	* Relation atoms (`=` `<` `⊏`) = `sep [lhs-term <+> glyph, rhs-term]`: glyph
457	  attached to the lhs line, rhs drops to the atom origin (probe:q_l4 tw1–3).
458	* Action atom = `hsep [fact, "@", timepoint]`: `@ tp` NEVER drops alone —
459	  at overflow the FACT breaks internally (its `)` drops to the fact column,
460	  `@ tp` beside it: `) @ #i` — probe:q_l5 m63/m64 pins hsep over the
461	  sep-alternative, which would have kept `fact ) @` on one line).
462	* One-line fits follow the engine at 110/73 with the ribbon measured from
463	  the nest; the closing quote/parens attached after a formula count against
464	  its last line's fit (probe:q_l5 — 73-content + `"` broke).
465	
466	## Restriction blocks (R3)
467	
468	```
469	restriction Name:
470	  "formula"                (nest 2; quotes attach directly around the doc)
471	  // safety formula        (col 2 — iff the formula classifies safety)
472	<blank>
473	  /*                       (comment at nest 2)
474	  expanded formula:
475	  "formula"
476	  */
477	```
478	
479	* The `axiom` keyword echoes as `restriction` with the identical wrapper
480	  (probe:q_ax1, target:Cronto_EA).
481	* **Statement vs expanded formula (R5 GAP-1).** The STATEMENT renders in MACRO
482	  form (as written); the `expanded formula:` comment renders in the
483	  macro/predicate-EXPANDED form. They are TWO DISTINCT formula values whenever
484	  the restriction uses a macro (target:MacroInLemmasAndRestrictions — statement
485	  `A( m(m3(x)) )`, expanded `A( x )`). PREDICATE expansion happens upstream of
486	  BOTH renderings, so a predicate-only restriction still shows the two
487	  identically (probe:q_pred1, target:features_predicates_minimal), and every
488	  macro-free restriction renders the same formula twice (the earlier "byte-
489	  identical in every observation" law — a special case, since no earlier probe
490	  had a macro). The expanded formula is a caller-supplied opaque input (the
491	  ported macro expansion), modeled as `Restriction.expanded`; safety is
492	  classified on the statement (macro expansion is term-level, so quantifier
493	  structure — hence the safety verdict — is identical either way).
494	* LEMMAS with macros have NO separate expanded-formula comment: the STATEMENT
495	  is the macro form (`l.formula`) and the guarded comment carries the EXPANDED
496	  form as opaque input (already handled by `Guarded`) — confirmed for
497	  exists-trace (target:MacroInLemmasAndRestrictions) and all-traces
498	  (probe:r5_allmacro: statement `Ev( g(x) )`, guarded `Ev( h(x) )`). No lemma
499	  model change was needed.
500	* Safety classification (pinned by probes q_s1/q_s2 + q_w1 + 84-block corpus
501	  parity): a formula is safety iff its negation-normal form contains NO
502	  existential quantifier, msg-sort and temporal alike. Every ⇒-antecedent
503	  flips polarity; `¬∃` in a conclusion (∀ in NNF) keeps safety, `¬∃` in an
504	  antecedent (∃ in NNF) defeats it; a non-temporal `∃ y.` conclusion defeats
505	  it; top-level `¬(∃ …)` restrictions are safety (target:Yubikey).
506	* Sort-order of the comment lines: statement, safety line, blank, comment —
507	  the safety line sits BETWEEN the formula and the blank (probe:q_w1 r_eq).
508	
509	## Lemma blocks (R3)
510	
511	```
512	lemma Name [attr1, attr2]:
513	  all-traces|exists-trace "formula"    (sep at nest 2 — one line iff it fits)
514	/*                                     (comment at col 0)
515	guarded formula characterizing all counter-examples:      (| all satisfying traces:)
516	"<guarded block>"                      (opaque input, verbatim lines)
517	*/
518	by sorry                               (| the embedded proof, verbatim)
519	```
520	
521	* Header: `lemma Name:`; with attributes `lemma Name [` + fill + `]:` — a
522	  SPACE before `[` (unlike rule attributes), items comma+space separated,
523	  fill-wrapped aligned after the `[` with `]:` attached to the last item;
524	  the first item stays beside the `[` even past the width (target:5G_AKA
525	  weakagreement_… at 120 display columns).
526	* Attributes render in SOURCE order, duplicates kept — NO canonicalization
527	  (probe:q_la1 `[hide_lemma=la1, use_induction, hide_lemma=la3, reuse]`).
528	  Spellings: `sources`, `reuse`, `use_induction`, `hide_lemma=<name>`,
529	  `heuristic=<value>` with the goal-ranking value verbatim (`S`, `{mytac}` —
530	  probes q_la2/q_la3; corpus `{sqn}` etc.).
531	* The trace-quantifier keyword is ALWAYS on the statement line, never the
532	  header line, even for `"⊤"` (probe:q_w1 l_top).
533	* Guarded comment at col 0, directly after the statement (no blank line):
534	  header `guarded formula characterizing all counter-examples:` for
535	  all-traces, `… all satisfying traces:` for exists-trace (probe:q_w1). The
536	  quoted content is OPAQUE pre-computed input (the ported guarded transform's
537	  rendering — note its style differs from the statement printer: n-ary ∧
538	  rows, lone `∧`/`⇒` lines, parenthesized ∃-body atoms; never produced by
539	  this crate).
540	* Failed conversion variant (probe:q_r1): header
541	  `conversion to guarded formula failed:` followed by the transform's error
542	  text with every line indented +2 (the raw error spelling, observed
543	  unindented in the fatal-restriction case, gains exactly 2 columns here).
544	* Tail: `by sorry` at col 0 when the source had no proof; a source-embedded
545	  proof re-renders after the `*/` instead (target:Yubikey `induction … qed`)
546	  — that proof text is PORTED-renderer output, carried verbatim as input.
547	* Restrictions outside the guarded fragment are a FATAL load error
548	  (probe:q_p1 raw), so the restriction renderer never sees them; lemmas
549	  outside it load fine and take the failed-conversion comment (probe:q_r1).
550	
551	## Theory frame (R5 GAP-3)
552	
553	Whole-echo assembly `theory <name> begin … end`. Curated byte targets: the
554	round1-3 `targets/*.hs.txt` whole captures; 29 diverse files
555	(tests/round5_theory.rs `whole_echo_frame_parity`) byte-verified end-to-end
556	(builtins variety, macros, rules ± variants, loop breakers, SAPIC process
557	attrs, restrictions ± safety ± expanded, lemmas all/exists ± attrs ± embedded
558	proof, predicates, heuristic, tactic, section comments).
559	
560	* **Header / footer.** Always `theory NAME` then a blank, `begin`, a blank
561	  (all 69 surveyed captures). No `configuration` string observed. Close: the
562	  extracted echo ALWAYS ends with exactly THREE blank lines then `end` (all 44
563	  surveyed captures). Those blanks are the residue the gate extraction leaves
564	  after dropping the two trailing comment blocks that are OUT of this crate's
565	  span — the wellformedness report and the `Generated from:` stamp — each a
566	  single blank-separated slot, plus the blank before `end` (RAW tail of
567	  target:MacroInLemmasAndRestrictions verified: `by sorry` · blank · wf-line ·
568	  blank · Generated-block · blank · `end`).
569	* **Signature first.** The signature block (`// Function signature …` + blank +
570	  `builtins:`/`functions:`/`equations:`) is ALWAYS the first thing after
571	  `begin`, even ahead of `tactic:` blocks (target:5G_AKA) and `heuristic:`
572	  (target:contract). It is rendered from the `Signature`, never a theory item.
573	* **Item order + spacing.** All other blocks are theory items in SOURCE order;
574	  the frame stacks them with ONE blank line between successive items. Item
575	  types observed: `macros:`, `predicate:` (grouped), rules, restrictions,
576	  lemmas, `heuristic:`, `tactic:`, `section{* … *}` formal comments, and the
577	  theory-level `/* looping facts with injective instances: … */` note.
578	  Assembly = `join(["theory NAME", "begin", sig, items…], "\n\n") + "\n\n\n\nend"`.
579	* **`heuristic:` line.** A single `heuristic: <value>` item (targets contract,
580	  ct, running-example, accountability — value e.g. `p`, `o "oracle" …`).
581	* **`predicate:` block.** One `predicate: <fact><=><formula>` per predicate; a
582	  contiguous run renders as one item, blank-line separated. NO spaces around
583	  `<=>`; the head is the R2 fact (`True( x )`), the body the R3 formula. The
584	  body wraps at ABSOLUTE MARGIN 0 (column-1 nest), INDEPENDENT of the header
585	  width (target:dmn-basic — `Sender_duplicate` and `Mixer_duplicate` bodies
586	  both wrap at col 1 despite different name lengths, and a 66-column body row
587	  fits ribbon 73 measured from col 1, not from the `<=>` column). A
588	  `<>`-beside composition would instead indent the body under `<=>` — the
589	  sanctioned HughesPJ `display` threads the current line width into each
590	  `NilAbove` continuation (`lay2 k (NilAbove p) = nl `txt` lay k p`;
591	  pretty-1.1.3.6 confirmed) — so the renderer splices the header textually onto
592	  the margin-0 formula's first line instead of `<>`-ing it.
593	* **Opaque / verbatim items.** `tactic:` (the whole contiguous region — sub-
594	  tactics `presort:`/`prio:` carry NO blank-line separators, so the region is
595	  one block; target:5G_AKA/alethea), `section{* … *}` formal comments
596	  (target:Joux/Yubikey/contract), and top-level `/* … */` comments (looping-
597	  facts note) are carried through the frame VERBATIM — their interior layout is
598	  outside this crate's erasure surface, like guarded/proof text.
599	
600	## UNOBSERVABLE (recorded per protocol, not guessed as pinned behavior)
601	
602	* PatMatch (`=x` pattern-match marker, sapic): cannot be forced through the
603	  MSR no-prove echo. Code renders `=` + term as a placeholder, flagged to
604	  this entry; must be pinned before any sapic-surface parity is claimed.
605	* SortHint::Suffix(s): the echo only ever shows sigil form (source `x:fresh`
606	  echoes `~x`, target:cav13), so suffix-tagged vars are rendered with the
607	  equivalent sigil.
608	* exp/mult element-level break choice: wide mult probes exceed the
609	  variant-computation budget (probe:t_mult timed out; 4-way
610	  probe:t_xorwide too). Union (probe:t_uniwide, round-3 target:alethea `)`
611	  drop) and Xor (probe:t_xorwide3, 3-way with long names: break after `⊕`
612	  attached to the preceding element, continuation after the `(`) pin the
613	  fill construction directly; Mult/NatPlus/exp-chains use the same
614	  construction by structural analogy — flagged, to be confirmed by the
615	  full-corpus gate at integration.
616	* reliable-channel builtin: requires a sapic top-level process (tool refuses
617	  otherwise — probe:b_reliable-channel raw); its signature contribution is
618	  unpinned in R1.
619	* Adapter contract notes: `NatLit`/`Number` carry DIGITS (renderer prefixes
620	  `%`); `NumberOne` is the DH `one`; `NatOne` is `%1`.
621	* Atom::LessMset (multiset smaller): no corpus witness and no reachable
622	  source spelling found (`(<)` absent from the whole corpus); rendered like
623	  `Less` as a flagged placeholder — must be pinned before any claim.
624	* Atom::Pred (predicate fact atoms): predicates are expanded upstream of the
625	  echo in BOTH the restriction statement and the expanded-formula comment
626	  (probe:q_pred1) and in sapic lemmas (corpus timepoints.spthy), so a Pred
627	  atom never reaches the no-prove renderer; rendered as a bare fact,
628	  flagged.
629	* Diff-mode surfaces (`--diff`): diffLemma items, lemma/restriction
630	  `left`/`right` attributes, `diff_reuse`. The parity corpus EXCLUDES all
631	  --diff files (RS cannot load them), so these never reach the gate; not
632	  modeled in the crate AST. A later round must pin them before any
633	  diff-file parity is claimed.
634	* Lemma modulo annotation (`lemma (modulo E) …`): never appears in any
635	  round-3 capture (grep across all 20); not modeled.
636	* Lemma attributes beyond the observed five (`output=…`, `Hint`, prover
637	  internals): no corpus/probe witness under the no-prove echo; not modeled.
638	* Fact annotations (`[+]`/`[-]`/`[no_precomp]`) on ACTION atoms inside
639	  formulas: never observed in any formula echo; the test parser rejects
640	  them loudly if they ever appear.
641	* ⇔ inside a safety-relevant restriction: no witness (⇔ restrictions are
642	  rare-to-absent in the corpus); the classifier treats each ⇔ side as
643	  occurring in both polarities (the NNF-consistent reading), flagged.
644	* Guarded-formula content and embedded proof scripts are PORTED-renderer
645	  output consumed verbatim as input — their interior layout is not a claim
646	  of this crate (only the comment frame and headers are).
647	* Restrictions whose formula falls outside the guarded fragment: fatal
648	  upstream error (probe:q_p1), unreachable for the renderer.
649

1	//! pretty-clean: a clean-room reimplementation of the tamarin-prover theory
2	//! pretty-printer (the `theory <name> begin … end` echo), derived purely from
3	//! black-box oracle behavior. See ../../SPEC.md for the observable boundary and
4	//! the R1–R4 sub-target decomposition, and workspace/BEHAVIOR.md for the
5	//! inferred behavioral spec you build up as you probe the oracle.
6	//!
7	//! Implemented sub-targets: R1 (term core + signature block), R2 (rule
8	//! blocks + macros surface), R3 (restriction / lemma / formula blocks), R4
9	//! (macros / predicates blocks), and R5 — the top-level `theory <name> begin
10	//! … end` frame (`theory::render`), the restriction statement/expanded-formula
11	//! split, and the always-break macros layout.
12	
13	pub mod ast;
14	pub mod doc; // BSD HughesPJ Doc engine — reuse graphdot's clean-room port.
15	
16	pub mod formula; // R3
17	pub mod lemma; // R3
18	pub mod macros; // R4
19	pub mod rule; // R2
20	pub mod signature; // R1
21	pub mod term; // R1 (deep core)
22	pub mod theory; // top-level assembly
23	
24	pub use ast::*;
25	
26	/// Whole theory echo: `theory <name> begin … end`, minus the wf comment and
27	/// the Generated-from stamp (both appended by the caller / stripped by the
28	/// gate). Top-level assembly of the R1–R4 sub-targets. See `theory::render`.
29	pub fn render_theory(thy: &ast::Theory, sig: &ast::Signature) -> String {
30	    theory::render(thy, sig)
31	}
32	
33	/// R1: the signature section — header comment, blank line, then the
34	/// `builtins:` / `functions:` / `equations:` declarations. See
35	/// `signature::render`.
36	pub fn render_signature_block(sig: &ast::Signature) -> String {
37	    signature::render(sig)
38	}
39	
40	/// R1: one term, rendered at the echo's layout parameters. See `term::render`.
41	pub fn render_term(term: &ast::Term) -> String {
42	    term::render(term)
43	}
44	
45	/// R4: the `macros:` block. See `macros::render_macros`.
46	pub fn render_macros(macros: &[ast::Macro]) -> String {
47	    macros::render_macros(macros)
48	}
49	
50	/// R4: a contiguous run of `predicate:` declarations. See
51	/// `macros::render_predicates`.
52	pub fn render_predicates(preds: &[ast::Predicate]) -> String {
53	    macros::render_predicates(preds)
54	}
55	
56	/// R2: one whole rule block — header, body, blank line, loop-breaker line,
57	/// and the AC-variants comment (`None` renders the trivial-variant
58	/// one-liner). See `rule::render`.
59	pub fn render_rule(r: &ast::Rule, variants: Option<&ast::AcVariants>) -> String {
60	    rule::render(r, variants)
61	}
62	
63	/// R2: one fact (`!Name( a, b )[+]`). See `rule::render_fact`.
64	pub fn render_fact(f: &ast::Fact) -> String {
65	    rule::render_fact(f)
66	}
67	
68	/// R3: one trace formula in top-level (bare) position. See `formula::render`.
69	pub fn render_formula(f: &ast::Formula) -> String {
70	    formula::render(f)
71	}
72	
73	/// R3: one whole restriction block (statement, conditional safety comment,
74	/// expanded-formula comment). See `lemma::render_restriction`.
75	pub fn render_restriction(r: &ast::Restriction) -> String {
76	    lemma::render_restriction(r)
77	}
78	
79	/// R3: one whole lemma block (header, statement, guarded-formula comment,
80	/// proof tail). See `lemma::render_lemma`.
81	pub fn render_lemma(l: &ast::Lemma, guarded: Option<&ast::Guarded>) -> String {
82	    lemma::render_lemma(l, guarded)
83	}

No prover source is read. Every claim traces to a probe 6 or a round-1 capture (`round1/targets/*.html`, themselves oracle output). 7 8 Terms: a **producer** renders a pre-computed prover value into response-body 9 CONTENT; the surrounding dispatch/route/envelope machinery is already 10 clean-roomed (`web-clean`). **Opaque content** = a pretty-printed string the 11 prover produced (formula / rule / signature / method text) that the producer 12 embeds but does not compute. 13 14 The acceptance gate (`scripts/web_parity.sh`) is **semantic / structural**, not 15 byte-identity: it canonicalizes away highlight `hl_*` spans, `
`, ` `, 16 attribute/JSON-key order, and volatile env fields, then compares element 17 structure, visible text, link hrefs, form actions, headings and JSON values. Pinned from BOTH sides: the corpus href inventory 221 [S14][S15] (render) and the live acceptance batteries [L08]–[L13] (parse). 222 223 **Segment model.** Split the raw tail on `/` FIRST, then percent-decode each 224 segment independently: `me%73sage` reaches `message`; an encoded `%2F` does NOT 225 split (`proof/foo%2F_` names lemma `foo/_`) [L09]. Decoding: valid `%XX` → the 226 byte, byte string read as UTF-8 with U+FFFD replacement (`caf%C3%A9`→café, 227 `a%FFb`→`a�b`); INVALID sequences stay literal (`a%zzb`, `a%`, `a%2`, `a%G1b`); 228 `+` is NOT a space [L12]. Heads match exactly and case-sensitively (`MESSAGE`, 229 `cases/RAW` → 404) [L08][L09]. 230 231 **Acceptance.** Heads: `help` `message` `rules` `tactic` (no args) · 232 `cases/{raw|refined}/{i}/{j}` · `lemma/{name}` · `proof/{lemma}[/seg…]` · 233 `edit/{name}` `add/{pos}` `delete/{name}`. Extra segments after a complete 234 match are IGNORED (`help/extra`, `message/`, `cases/raw/0/0/extra` accepted); 235 missing required args reject (`proof`, `cases/raw/1` → 404); name args accept 236 ANY decoded text including empty (`edit/`, `proof//_` parse); lemma EXISTENCE 237 is resolution, not parse (`proof/nonexistent` → 200 "No such lemma or proof 238 path") [L08][L11]. `sources` is NOT a head; `method/{lemma}/{n}[/…]` is 239 server-accepted but outside the producer link vocabulary (no ThyPath 240 constructor → `parse` returns None; documented interface scope). 241 242 **Numeric segments** (the two `cases` indices) parse as a Haskell-`reads`-shaped 243 integer prefix [L10]: optional whitespace, balanced parens, optional `-` (space 244 allowed after), one integer lexeme — decimal / `0x` `0o` `0b` any case — then 245 arbitrary junk IGNORED at top level (`1abc`, `1_`, `(1)x` accept) but not inside 246 parens (`(1x)` rejects); a decimal lexeme continuing as a FLOAT rejects (`1.0`, 247 `1e2`); a LEADING underscore rejects (`_1` lexes as an identifier — the 248 underscore-prefix quirk) while interior/trailing ones are junk (`1_0`, `0x_1`). 249 `+1` and `--1` reject. The VALUE is behaviorally inert (bodies for 0/0, 0/1, 250 1/0, 9/9, -1/0 are byte-identical [L10]) — which index is source vs case is 251 UNOBSERVABLE; clean impl clamps out-of-usize values (documented choice). The 252 VERSION-index segment before the handler is a different, stricter grammar 253 (`01`,`+1` accepted; `0x1`, spaces rejected [L10]) and is out of R5 (producers 254 render it as plain decimal — all hrefs carry plain decimals [S14]). 255 256 **Render.** Corpus-wide, rendered segments contain only `[A-Za-z0-9_.]` raw 257 plus the single escape pair `%3C`/`%3E` in `add/%3Cfirst%3E` (946 corpus + live 258 hrefs). Encoding of any OTHER byte is UNOBSERVABLE — the metachar-filename 259 channel collapsed (download/get_and_append URLs derive from the theory NAME, 260 not the filename [L13]) — clean impl: RFC3986 unreserved raw, everything else 261 uppercase `%XX` per UTF-8 byte (reproduces every observed href byte; gated by 262 the 40037-distinct-tail corpus round-trip [S15]). 263 264 ## 12. R2 — the west (proof-script) pane frame (`src/proofscript.rs`) 265 266 The pane is the content of the page's proof-script container: logical lines 267 each emitted as `TEXT
\n` (leading spaces → ` `, i.e. the §3 268 postprocess) plus ONE trailing space — all 478 overview captures (82 help + 269 396 proof views; the SPEC's 473 undercounts) [S16]. Element order [S16]: 270 271 1. `theory NAME begin` (keyword spans; NAME is a `main/help` link); 272 2. per nav item: blank, then 273 `LABEL ANN` 274 — exactly five, fixed order message / rules / tactic / cases/raw/0/0 / 275 cases/refined/0/0. LABEL+ANN are opaque input: `Message theory`/`Tactic(s)` 276 with empty ANN (leaving ` `), rules `Multiset rewriting rules` 277 ± ` and restrictions` (varies with the theory, unlike the R1 title) with 278 `(count)`, `Raw sources`/`Refined sources ` (trailing space in the label) 279 with the cases description; 280 3. blank + the `add lemma` link for `add/%3Cfirst%3E`; 281 4. per lemma: blank · declaration (§13) · the quantifier/formula block (§13) · 282 `edit lemma or delete lemma` 283 (two spaces around `or`) · the proof display · blank · 284 `add lemma` → `add/NAME`; 285 5. blank + `end`. ZERO lemmas leave TWO blanks before `end` (both lemma-less 286 corpus panes). 287 288 **Proof display.** Unproven: the single line `by sorry` (keyword spans), no 290 header wrapper. Proved/disproved: the lemma HEADER (declaration through the 291 delete anchor) is wrapped in ONE status span — `hl_good` ×3192 / `hl_bad` ×146 292 — opening before the declaration and closing right after the delete anchor; 293 the proof lines follow UNWRAPPED, structured by the §16 tree grammar (the 294 wrapper class is the tree ROOT's status). An INCOMPLETE proof (root step 295 `sorry-step`, e.g. a half-done induction) leaves the header unwrapped like 296 sorry [S16]. Every href is 297 `/thy/trace/{idx}/main/` + an R5-rendered path. 298 299 ## 13. R2 — lemma declaration + formula layout 300 301 Declaration: `lemma NAME{ATTRS}:` — ATTRS empty or starting `" ["` (observed 302 vocabulary: reuse / use_induction / sources / heuristic={…} / hide_lemma=… and 303 combinations), and possibly MULTI-LINE (46 corpus declarations wrap long 304 heuristic lists; the continuation indent is baked into the opaque ATTRS text) 305 [S17]. The `:` ends the declaration. 306 307 Quantifier/formula block at indent 2. A SINGLE-line formula inlines onto the 308 quantifier line (` all-traces "F"`) iff the assembled line's 309 ESCAPED width is ≤ 69, where escaped width = character count with tags 310 stripped and entities counted at their escaped length (`<` = 4, `"` = 311 6, unicode operators 1 each). Otherwise the quantifier stands alone and each 312 formula line follows at 2 + its own relative indent. Provenance: visible 313 chars/bytes DO NOT separate the corpus (minimal pair at 55 visible: `(a++a)` 314 inline vs `` vertical [S18]); escaped chars separate (65 vs 71), and the 315 live WProbe bisection pinned the boundary to exactly 69/70 on four formula 316 families, ruling out a byte-based metric [L14]. Quantifier vocabulary observed: 317 `all-traces` / `exists-trace`. 318 319 ## 14. Round-2 validation status 320 321 R5 is `path.rs` (`parse`/`render`), R2 is `proofscript.rs` (`render_index`, 322 links via R5), validated by: 323 - `tests/r5_path_grammar.rs` — live acceptance battery replay (68 accepted + 324 27 rejected probes [L08]–[L12]), decode-echo fixtures, parse⇄render 325 round-trip, and the corpus sweep: all 40037 distinct `main/*` href tails 326 re-render byte-identically (497 `method/` tails asserted out-of-vocabulary). 327 - `tests/r2_west_pane.rs` — `corpus_sweep_all_overview_panes`: all 478 pane 328 bodies sliced (strict inversion asserting every frame byte) and re-rendered 329 byte-identically; `live_probe_pane_replays`: 3 panes from never-captured 330 theories (PathProbe fresh; WProbe, the 35-lemma width-boundary theory; 331 PathProbe v2 after a LIVE autoprove — proved `hl_good` tree) [L15]; fixtures 332 pinning the frame + zero-lemma spacing. 333 - Mutation checks (all observed to fail, then reverted): ` or `→` or ` 334 breaks corpus+live+fixtures; width 69→68 breaks the corpus sweep while 335 69→70 breaks ONLY the live WProbe replay (the live bisection pins what the 336 corpus cannot); uppercase→lowercase `%XX` breaks the R5 byte tests. 337 - `cargo test`: 24 green; `cargo clippy --all-targets`: zero warnings. 338 339 ## 15. Round-2 unobservables 340 341 - UNOBSERVABLE (documented choices): href %-encoding beyond `%3C/%3E` (§11); 342 which cases index is source vs case (§11); escaping of nav-item 343 labels/annotations and attribute text (metachar-free in all observations — 344 passed through opaque; lemma/theory NAMES escaped uniformly like R1); 345 formula layout for a single-line formula at widths the corpus/live probes 346 cannot reach is fixed by the ≤69 rule [L14]. 347 348 --- 349 350 ## 16. R3 — the proof-tree line grammar (`src/prooftree.rs`) 351 352 A lemma's proof display is a TREE of method-labelled nodes rendered as 353 logical lines in the west pane (then §3-postprocessed with everything else). 354 Pinned from the 478-pane corpus [S19]–[S21] plus live forcing on own 355 theories [L16]–[L18]. Per node at indent `d`, URL path `P` = `proof/{lemma}` 356 + one segment per case on the root-to-node walk (`_` for an unnamed 357 continuation; case display names == href segments 1:1, all `[A-Za-z0-9_]+` 358 in observation — names rendered via the R1 escape + R5 segment encoding): 359 360 ``` 361 step : {d spaces}[BY]{STEP}[REMOVE] 362 BY = wrap(S, 'by ') — iff the node has 363 NO cases and is not a terminal MARKER (SOLVED-style lines never 364 carry `by`; contradiction / zero-case solve / sorry always do) 365 STEP = METHOD 366 (CLS = hl_good | hl_bad | sorry-step-for-status-less) 367 | wrap(S, METHOD) — Replayed nodes: 368 no proof-step link, the method sits in the status span 369 REMOVE = — on every 370 step EXCEPT the sorry slots (status-less nodes whose only 371 continuation, if any, is replayed); replayed leftovers KEEP it 372 ``` 373 374 - a single unnamed case continues at the SAME indent (segment `_`), no 375 case/next/qed framing; 376 - named cases (1 or more): per case `{d+2}wrap(S_child, 'case NAME')`, the 377 child subtree at `d+2`; siblings separated by `{d}wrap(S_parent, 'next')`; 378 the block closes `{d}wrap(S_parent, 'qed')`. `next`/`qed` carry the 379 PARENT's status — pinned against prev-case (x117) / following-case (x99) 380 alternatives corpus-wide [S19] and by the live mixed tree where the case 381 after a bad-parent `next` is GOOD [L18]; the case line carries the 382 CHILD's status (426612/426612 [S19]). 383 - `wrap(S, x)` = `x` for a statused node, bare `x` 384 for a status-less one. Status→class: Good→hl_good, Bad→hl_bad, 385 Replayed→hl_superfluous [S20]; Medium→hl_medium is an ASSUMED name (§18). 386 - METHOD text is opaque pre-rendered input (keyword/operator/comment spans, 387 possibly MULTI-LINE with continuation indents baked in — the anchor and 388 the superfluous span both straddle physical lines). 389 - The lemma HEADER wrapper (§12) is the ROOT's status class; a status-less 390 root (incomplete proof) leaves it unwrapped [S16][L17]. 391 - The R2 `by sorry` line (§12) is exactly this grammar on a status-less 392 sorry leaf; `ProofDisplay::Unproven` remains as the convenience form. 393 394 ## 17. R3 — statuses and link modes (observed matrix) 395 396 | node | anchor class | by-wrap | remove-step | 397 |------|--------------|---------|-------------| 398 | proven step (leaf or interior) | `proof-step hl_good` | `hl_good` | yes | 399 | attack-path step | `proof-step hl_bad` | `hl_bad` | yes | 400 | incomplete interior (real children) | `proof-step sorry-step` | — | yes [L17] | 401 | sorry leaf (incl. `/* bound N hit */`, `/* invalid proof step encountered */`) | `proof-step sorry-step` | bare | NO | 402 | invalid-step sorry carrying a replayed leftover | `proof-step sorry-step` | none (has a child) | NO [S20][L18] | 403 | replayed leftover step | no anchor; method in `hl_superfluous` span | `hl_superfluous` | yes [S20][L18] | 404 405 Crate model: `ProofTree { method_text, status, live, terminal_marker, 406 cases }` — `live` gates the remove-step (false exactly on the sorry slots), 407 `terminal_marker` marks the SOLVED-style terminal lines (never `by`; the 408 interface header carries no such field — the adapter derives it from the 409 step kind, as it derives `live` from sorry-ness). `ProofDisplay::Rendered` 410 (round-2 opaque lines) is REPLACED by `ProofDisplay::Tree`. 411 412 ## 18. R3 unobservables (recorded per protocol) 413 414 - `hl_medium` NEVER occurs (corpus census [S21]; live attempts: bounded 415 autoprove [L17], characterize mixed tree [L18] — both render without it). 416 The Medium→`hl_medium` class name is an assumed pattern extension. 417 - A live (anchored) Replayed node, a non-live Good/Bad node, and a 418 status-less NON-live interior are unobserved; the renderer keys the 419 span-vs-anchor choice on Replayed status and the remove-step on `live`. 420 - Equiv-kind (`/thy/equiv/`) panes: no equiv theory in the corpus and the 421 oracle wrapper cannot pass `--diff` — proof-step hrefs are rendered with 422 the observed `trace` kind only. 423 - A case literally NAMED `_`, an empty case name among named siblings, and 424 multiple unnamed siblings never occur (`_` segments are only ever inline 425 continuations [S14][S21]). 426 - The superfluous replay drops solver comments from method text (live 427 `contradiction` leftover lost `/* from formulas */` [L18]) — method text 428 is input; nothing for the renderer. 429 430 ## 19. R4 — the welcome/index page + housekeeping (`src/welcome.rs`) 431 432 No non-`/thy` body exists in the capture corpus — all pinned live 433 [L19]–[L21]. The `/` page is a fixed frame (verbatim captured segments, 434 including the doubled `` closers; NO trailing newline) 435 with three slots: 436 437 1. FLASH — first child of ``: absent on GET; 438 `

Loaded new theory!

` after a successful upload; 439 `

Post request failed.

` for a POST without a 440 usable file; the entity-escaped multi-line load-error text otherwise 441 (raw newlines kept) [L20]. 442 2. VERSION — `Running Tamarin ` 443 + the version text in the north header [L19]. 444 3. ROWS — between the fixed `` and ``, one per version, 445 index ascending: 446 `NAMETIME 447 Original|ModifiedORIGIN` — the Modified 448 cell's `` is UNCLOSED (reproduced byte-exactly); NAME/TIME/ORIGIN 449 opaque, entity-escaped by the producer (metachar-filename upload [L21]); 450 ORIGIN = load path for a disk theory, bare uploaded filename for an 451 upload; initial load = Original, every derived/uploaded version = 452 Modified [L19][L20]. 453 454 Housekeeping bodies [L19]: `robots.txt` → `User-agent: *`; `/kill?path=…` 455 → `Canceled request!`; missing `/static` file → `File not found` (all 456 plain text, no trailing NL); `/kill` without a path → the 400 457 Invalid-Arguments page: the standard error shell around 458 `

Invalid Arguments

\n
    ` + `
  • MSG
    • \n` per message + 459 `
\n` (single observed instance: `No path to kill specified!`). 460 `/favicon.ico` is an empty-body 303 (dispatch-side, no producer body). 461 462 R4 unobservables: multi-item Invalid-Arguments lists (only the one-item 463 kill instance is reachable); equiv-kind row hrefs (no `--diff` oracle); 464 the index-page display cap on many versions is row SELECTION (adapter 465 state), not rendering. 466 467 ## 20. Round-3 validation status 468 469 R3 is `prooftree.rs` (render_tree_lines / render_tree, consumed by 470 `proofscript` for every ProofDisplay::Tree), R4 is `welcome.rs` 471 (render_welcome + render_invalid_args + the plain-text body constants), 472 validated by: 473 - tests/r2_west_pane.rs — the corpus sweep UPGRADED: every proof display in 474 all 478 panes parsed to a structured tree (frame bytes, status placement, 475 canonical hrefs asserted; only method text opaque) and re-rendered 476 byte-identically; the 3 round-2 live panes replay unchanged. 477 - tests/r3_proof_tree.rs — 4 live-probe pane replays (proved TreeProbe, 478 bounded TreeProbe, doctored ScriptProbe with a superfluous leftover, 479 mixed-status TreeProbe2 characterize) + fixtures pinning every line form. 480 - tests/r4_welcome.rs — 6 live index-page byte replays (strict slot 481 inversion) + housekeeping/Invalid-Arguments byte tests. 482 - cargo test: 35 green; cargo clippy --all-targets: zero warnings; std-only. 483 - Mutation checks (observed to fail, reverted): next→following-case status 484 breaks the corpus sweep + mixed replay; dropping the marker by-exception 485 breaks SOLVED lines everywhere; closing the row `` breaks all index 486 replays. 487
