1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Slice fragment families out of the captured response corpus.
3	
4	The captured corpus (`oracle/captured_responses/*.hs.json`, the sanctioned
5	channel PROTOCOL permits reading) holds, per theory, a crawl manifest:
6	
7	    { base, capped, lemmas, log, manifest: { "<url>": {kind,status,body} } }
8	
9	`<url>` carries the literal token `#` where the server's version index goes;
10	`kind` is one of html | text | json | dot; a `json` body is itself the JSON
11	envelope string `{"html":..,"title":..}` (or `{"redirect":..}`/`{"alert":..}`).
12	
13	This helper lets the sealed room materialize the *exact response bytes* for one
14	fragment family without re-crawling — the byte targets a producer must
15	reproduce. It reads ONLY the captured corpus (no corpus `.spthy`, no source
16	tree); it labels each manifest by the theory name it advertises plus a short
17	content hash so name-collisions (Joux, Scott, …) stay distinct.
18	
19	Usage:
20	  extract_fragments.py list                       # manifests: label / name / kinds
21	  extract_fragments.py families                    # URL families + counts + kinds
22	  extract_fragments.py extract <family[,family2,...]> <outdir> [--only SUBSTR[,SUBSTR2,...]]
23	        # <family> is a URL skeleton, idx-agnostic; pass several comma-separated
24	        # to slice them all in ONE corpus pass, e.g.:
25	        #   overview   main/message   main/rules   main/tactic   main/help
26	        #   main/lemma   source   next   main/cases   main/proof
27	        # writes one file per (manifest, matching-url): <label>__<slug>.<ext>
28	        # json envelopes also emit a sibling .title file (the pane title).
29	  --only SUBSTR  restrict to manifests whose label CONTAINS any listed SUBSTR
30	                 (comma-separated). Omit to take every manifest.
31	
32	All output files are captured program OUTPUT (legitimate byte targets); the
33	`.meta.tsv` index records label -> theory-name -> capture-hash for traceability.
34	"""
35	import json
36	import os
37	import re
38	import sys
39	
40	HERE = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
41	CORPUS = os.environ.get("CAPTURES", os.path.join(HERE, "captured_responses"))
42	
43	_IDX = re.compile(r"/thy/(trace|equiv)/[^/]+/")
44	
45	
46	def label_of(path, manifest):
47	    """theory-name + 8-char capture hash (collision-proof, capture-only)."""
48	    h = os.path.basename(path).split(".")[0][:8]
49	    name = None
50	    for url, r in manifest.items():
51	        if r.get("kind") == "html" and "/overview/" in url:
52	            m = re.search(r"<title>Theory:\s*([^<]*)</title>", r.get("body", ""))
53	            if m:
54	                name = m.group(1).strip()
55	                break
56	    if name is None:
57	        for url, r in manifest.items():
58	            if r.get("kind") == "text" and url.endswith("/source"):
59	                m = re.match(r"theory\s+(\S+)", r.get("body", ""))
60	                if m:
61	                    name = m.group(1)
62	                    break
63	    name = re.sub(r"[^A-Za-z0-9_.-]", "_", name or "UNKNOWN")
64	    return "%s__%s" % (name, h), name, h
65	
66	
67	def family_of(url):
68	    """idx-agnostic URL skeleton down to the handler section."""
69	    u = _IDX.sub("/thy/trace/#/", url)
70	    m = re.match(r"^/thy/trace/#/(main/[a-zA-Z]+|[a-zA-Z-]+)", u)
71	    if m:
72	        return m.group(1)
73	    return u.strip("/") or "root"
74	
75	
76	def slug(url):
77	    return re.sub(r"[^A-Za-z0-9]+", "_", _IDX.sub("/thy/trace/#/", url)).strip("_")
78	
79	
80	def manifests():
81	    for p in sorted(__import__("glob").glob(os.path.join(CORPUS, "*.hs.json"))):
82	        try:
83	            yield p, json.load(open(p))
84	        except Exception as e:  # noqa
85	            print("skip %s: %s" % (p, e), file=sys.stderr)
86	
87	
88	def cmd_list():
89	    for p, d in manifests():
90	        m = d.get("manifest", {})
91	        lbl, name, h = label_of(p, m)
92	        kinds = sorted({r.get("kind") for r in m.values()})
93	        print("%-40s  %-28s  urls=%-5d  kinds=%s" % (lbl, name, len(m), ",".join(kinds)))
94	
95	
96	def cmd_families():
97	    import collections
98	    fam = collections.Counter()
99	    famk = collections.defaultdict(set)
100	    for _, d in manifests():
101	        for url, r in d.get("manifest", {}).items():
102	            f = family_of(url)
103	            fam[f] += 1
104	            famk[f].add(r.get("kind"))
105	    for f, c in sorted(fam.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1]):
106	        print("%6d  %-24s  %s" % (c, f, ",".join(sorted(famk[f]))))
107	
108	
109	def _write(outdir, base, body, title=None):
110	    os.makedirs(outdir, exist_ok=True)
111	    ext = "html" if ("<" in body or title is not None) else "txt"
112	    with open(os.path.join(outdir, base + "." + ext), "w") as fh:
113	        fh.write(body)
114	    if title is not None:
115	        with open(os.path.join(outdir, base + ".title"), "w") as fh:
116	            fh.write(title)
117	
118	
119	def cmd_extract(family, outdir, only=None):
120	    fams = set(f.strip() for f in family.split(",") if f.strip())
121	    subs = [s.strip() for s in only.split(",")] if only else None
122	    n = 0
123	    meta = []
124	    for p, d in manifests():
125	        m = d.get("manifest", {})
126	        lbl, name, h = label_of(p, m)
127	        if subs and not any(s in lbl for s in subs):
128	            continue
129	        for url, r in m.items():
130	            if family_of(url) not in fams:
131	                continue
132	            body = r.get("body", "")
133	            title = None
134	            if r.get("kind") == "json":
135	                try:
136	                    env = json.loads(body)
137	                except Exception:
138	                    env = None
139	                if isinstance(env, dict) and "html" in env:
140	                    title = env.get("title", "")
141	                    body = env["html"]
142	                # {redirect}/{alert} envelopes are left as raw JSON (no html key).
143	            base = "%s__%s" % (lbl, slug(url))
144	            _write(outdir, base, body, title)
145	            meta.append((base, name, h, url, r.get("kind"), r.get("status")))
146	            n += 1
147	    with open(os.path.join(outdir, ".meta.tsv"), "w") as fh:
148	        fh.write("target\ttheory\thash\turl\tkind\tstatus\n")
149	        for row in meta:
150	            fh.write("\t".join(str(x) for x in row) + "\n")
151	    print("wrote %d '%s' fragments to %s" % (n, family, outdir))
152	
153	
154	def main(argv):
155	    if not argv or argv[0] == "list":
156	        return cmd_list()
157	    if argv[0] == "families":
158	        return cmd_families()
159	    if argv[0] == "extract":
160	        family = argv[1]
161	        outdir = argv[2]
162	        only = None
163	        if "--only" in argv:
164	            only = argv[argv.index("--only") + 1]
165	        return cmd_extract(family, outdir, only)
166	    print(__doc__)
167	    return 2
168	
169	
170	if __name__ == "__main__":
171	    sys.exit(main(sys.argv[1:]) or 0)
172

#!/usr/bin/env python3 """Census the web-mode rendering from extracted main/message + main/rules bodies. Input: the .html files written by extract_fragments.py (decoded env["html"] fragments). We invert the producers skin postprocess to recover the BODY lines (the opaque content this round must produce), then census spans/escapes/width. """ import glob, os, re, sys, collections PANES = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)), "panes") P_OPEN = '

' def unpostprocess(html): assert html.endswith("
\n"), repr(html[-20:]) lines = html.split("
\n") assert lines[-1] == "" lines = lines[:-1] out = [] for line in lines: n = 0 while line.startswith(" "): line = line[6:] n += 1 out.append(" " * n + line) return out def parse_blocks(lines): """Return list of (heading, [body-lines]) and leading_blank bool.""" i = 0 leading_blank = bool(lines) and lines[0] == "" if leading_blank: i = 1 blocks = [] while i < len(lines): m = re.match(r"^

(.*)

$", lines[i]) assert m, "expected heading, got %r (idx %d)" % (lines[i], i) heading = m.group(1) i += 1 assert lines[i].startswith(P_OPEN), "expected p open, got %r" % lines[i] first = lines[i][len(P_OPEN):] body = [] cur = first while True: if cur.endswith("

"): stripped = cur[:-4] if not (stripped == "" and body == []): body.append(stripped) i += 1 break body.append(cur) i += 1 cur = lines[i] blocks.append((heading, body)) return leading_blank, blocks def strip_spans(s): return re.sub(r']*>', '', s) def unescape(s): return (s.replace("<","<").replace(">",">").replace(""",'"') .replace("'","'").replace("&","&")) def main(): what = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else "census" msg_files = sorted(glob.glob(os.path.join(PANES, "*main_message.html"))) rules_files = sorted(glob.glob(os.path.join(PANES, "*main_rules.html"))) if what == "census": span_classes = collections.Counter() # token census: for each span, record class + inner content span_inner = collections.defaultdict(collections.Counter) entities = collections.Counter() headings = collections.Counter() for f in msg_files + rules_files: html = open(f).read() lines = unpostprocess(html) lb, blocks = parse_blocks(lines) for h, body in blocks: headings[h] += 1 for bl in body: for m in re.finditer(r'(.*?)', bl): span_classes[m.group(1)] += 1 span_inner[m.group(1)][m.group(2)] += 1 for m in re.finditer(r'&[a-z#0-9]+;', bl): entities[m.group(0)] += 1 print("=== SPAN CLASSES ===") for c, n in span_classes.most_common(): print(" %-16s %d" % (c, n)) print("\n=== SPAN INNER CONTENT by class (distinct) ===") for c in span_inner: print("class=%s: %d distinct inners" % (c, len(span_inner[c]))) for inner, n in span_inner[c].most_common(60): print(" %6d %r" % (n, inner)) print("\n=== ENTITIES ===") for e, n in entities.most_common(): print(" %-10s %d" % (e, n)) print("\n=== HEADINGS ===") for h, n in headings.most_common(): print(" %-45s %d" % (h, n)) elif what == "width": # For each body line, strip spans + unescape -> visible plain text. # Report max visible length and the distribution near the top. maxlen = 0 maxline = None lens = collections.Counter() long_lines = [] for f in msg_files + rules_files: html = open(f).read() lines = unpostprocess(html) lb, blocks = parse_blocks(lines) for h, body in blocks: for bl in body: plain = unescape(strip_spans(bl)) L = len(plain) lens[L] += 1 if L > maxlen: maxlen = L; maxline = (os.path.basename(f), h, plain) if L >= 95: long_lines.append((L, os.path.basename(f)[:30], plain)) print("max visible plain length:", maxlen) print("max line:", maxline) print("\nlength distribution >=90:") for L in sorted(lens): if L >= 90: print(" len %3d : %d lines" % (L, lens[L])) print("\nlongest lines (>=95):") for L, fn, pl in sorted(long_lines, reverse=True)[:40]: print(" %3d %-30s |%s|" % (L, fn, pl)) if __name__ == "__main__": main()

186:data Doc a

239:data AnnotDetails a = AnnotStart
240:                    | NoAnnot !TextDetails {-# UNPACK #-} !Int

244:instance Functor AnnotDetails where
245:  fmap _ AnnotStart     = AnnotStart

250:annotSize :: AnnotDetails a -> Int

input.command: cat >> /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/pretty/workspace/BEHAVIOR.md <<'EOF' ## Web mode (R6) — the interactive server's HTML rendering of theory content The interactive server renders the SAME theory blocks the batch echo models, but as HTML fragment bodies for the `main/message` and `main/rules` panes (and the source views). Derived entirely from the 82 captured crawl manifests (weblayer/producers/oracle/captured_responses; sliced by extract_fragments.py into scratchpad/r6/panes) and doc-engine probes — no tamarin HS source read. The producers-clean skin (escape_text / postprocess_lines / `{html,title}` envelope, weblayer BEHAVIOR §2–4) consumes these bodies as OPAQUE input and wraps them; R6 PRODUCES the bodies. Acceptance composes R6 output through that skin and byte-compares the whole captured pane body. ### Layout parameters — width 100, ribbon 67 (NOT the batch 110/73) DEFINITIVE (scratchpad zz_width_probe, deleted): all 139 signature `functions:`/`builtins:` fills reconstructed from the corpus render byte-identical at `render_with(100, 67, doc)`; ribbon 66 misses 10, ribbon 68 misses 9. These are the default HughesPJ `Style` (`lineLength = 100`, `ribbonsPerLine = 1.5`, `round(100/1.5) = 67`); batch mode is the same 1.5 ratio at lineLength 110 (`round(110/1.5) = 73`). A fill item is kept on a line iff its END column ≤ the ribbon (probe:ribbon_boundary), but the fill has HughesPJ lookahead so this is NOT a per-item greedy rule — only the engine reproduces it. ### The escape set — identical to producers `escape_text` `& < > " '` → `& < > " '` (verified: the corpus entity inventory of message/rules bodies is exactly `' > < "` plus `&`; no `'`, no `\uXXXX`). Backslash and non-ASCII pass raw (the formula glyphs `∀ ∃ ⇒ ∧ ∨ ¬ ⊥ ⊏ ⊤ ⊐` are raw UTF-8). Leading indent spaces are NOT converted here — the producers postprocess turns them into ` ` runs. So an R6 body line carries: escaped content + `hl_*` spans + RAW leading spaces, one logical line per `\n`. ### The `hl_*` span vocabulary (census over all 82+82 bodies) `hl_keyword` — 8 token kinds, the span covers the KEYWORD ONLY (incl the trailing colon of a declaration keyword, but NOT any trailing space): * `rule`, `modulo` (every rule header AND every AC re-render inside a variants comment; two separate spans, the `(`/`)`/name between them PLAIN); * `variants` (the `variants (modulo AC)` line — `variants` spanned, the rest plain); * `restriction` (restriction header; name+`:` plain); * `functions:`, `equations:`, `builtins:`, `equations [convergent]:` (declaration keywords, colon INCLUDED). Rule NAME, attribute list and the attribute brackets `[color=…, process="…"]` are PLAIN (never spanned). `hl_operator` — 18 token kinds: * rule structure `[` `]` `--[` `]->`(`]->`) `-->`(`-->`); * formula connective/negation parens `(` `)` — ONLY the connective-operand and `¬`-argument parens; application `f(…)`, fact `Name( … )`, AC-operator `(a*b)` and pair `<…>` delimiters are NEVER spanned (pair/AC `<`/`>` are only entity-escaped); * `=` (equation `=` AND the `Eq` atom `=`), `<` (the `Less`/temporal atom, escaped `<`), `@` (action-at), `.` (quantifier dot after binders); * `∀ ` / `∃ ` (glyph + trailing space, one span), `⇒`, `∧`, `∨`, `¬`, `⊥`. Observed-absent, rendered by pattern and FLAGGED (no corpus witness): `⊤` (true), `⊏` (subterm), `⇔` (iff), `last(…)`, `⊐`. A variants-substitution `= ` (`~lv2 = ~lv2.4`) is PLAIN, not `hl_operator` (distinct from the equation `=`). Binders, timepoints and all terms/facts inside atoms are PLAIN. `hl_comment` — the whole comment is ONE span, tags need not balance per line: * `/* has exactly the trivial AC variant */` (single line); * the multi-line `/* … variants (modulo AC) … */` block: span OPENS at `/*`, CLOSES after `*/`, and the AC-rule re-render INSIDE it keeps its own nested `hl_keyword`/`hl_operator` spans (spans NEST); * the restriction `/* expanded formula: "…" */` block: same nesting (the `expanded formula:` text plain, the quoted formula's operator spans nested); * `// safety formula`, `// loop breaker: [n]`, `// loop breakers: [i,j]`. A comment's leading indent spaces are OUTSIDE the span (raw, before the open). ### Content structure vs the batch echo * NO signature header comment: the `Signature`/message body starts directly at `builtins:` / `functions:` (the batch `// Function signature …` line is absent). * `main/message` bodies (three Keep-mode sections, producers §5): `Signature` = the builtins/functions/equations block at web params, no header; `Construction Rules` / `Deconstruction Rules` = the intruder rules rendered BARE — header + body ONLY, NO variants comment / loop breaker — joined by ONE blank line. * `main/rules` bodies (producers §6): `Fact Symbols …` = one OPAQUE input line (`None`, or `L_x(id,?,?), …` with `?`/`=`/`.`/`≤`/`<` placeholder chars, entity-escaped, NO spans — carried through, not modeled); `Multiset Rewriting Rules` = FULL rule blocks (header + body + blank + `/* variant */`), joined by TWO blank lines; `Restrictions of the Set of Traces` = restriction blocks (statement, `// safety formula` when safety, blank, `/* expanded formula: */`), joined by one blank; `Macros` slot when present (UNOBSERVED in the 82-manifest corpus — needs a live macros theory). * The restriction wrapper quotes (`"…"`) are PLAIN; the formula's operator spans sit inside. Statement and expanded-formula render the two distinct formula values (batch R5 GAP-1) at web params. ### Implementation (pretty-clean, `web.rs` + span-site edits) One model, two render targets: the SAME block-doc builders (signature/rule/formula/lemma) emit glyphs through `hl_*` wrappers that, in web mode, wrap the glyph in a zero-width span-marker pair (mirroring the sanctioned `Annotated.HughesPJ` `AnnotStart`/`AnnotEnd`: `sized_text(0, …)` sentinels that flow through best/fits/lay at zero width, so LAYOUT is identical to plain), and in batch mode are the identity (byte-identical to R1–R5). `render_html` = `render_with(100, 67, doc)` then a post-pass that entity-escapes and expands the sentinels into ``/``. Multi-line comment spans fall out naturally (open marker before `/*`, close after `*/`, `\n`s between). No change to the doc engine. \n")[:-1]: n=0 while l.startswith(" "): l=l[6:]; n+=1 out.append(" "*n+l) return out def strip(s): return re.sub(r']*>','',s) P_OPEN='

' def bodies(html): lines=unpp(html); i=0 lb=bool(lines) and lines[0]=="" if lb: i=1 blocks=[] while i(.*)$",lines[i]); h=m.group(1); i+=1 first=lines[i][len(P_OPEN):]; body=[]; cur=first while True: if cur.endswith("

content.content:
1	//! Minimal AST subset the renderer consumes.
2	//!
3	//! This is the clean crate's OWN model, shaped to stay drop-in compatible with
4	//! the integration surface in `../../interface/ast_types.rs` (same variant
5	//! names and field meanings) while only carrying what the R1 renderer needs.
6	//! Behavior is driven by the oracle (see workspace/BEHAVIOR.md), not by that
7	//! header.
8	//!
9	//! R2–R4 types are placeholders — flesh them out at those sub-targets.
10	
11	// ── R1: terms (the deep core) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
12	
13	/// Multiset-rewriting term. Rendered by `term::render`.
14	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]
15	pub enum Term {
16	    Var(VarSpec),
17	    /// Public string constant; renders quoted: `'name'`.
18	    PubLit(String),
19	    /// Fresh name constant; renders `~'name'`.
20	    FreshLit(String),
21	    /// Natural-number literal, digits only; renders `%digits`.
22	    NatLit(String),
23	    /// Natural-number literal; renders `%n`.
24	    Number(u64),
25	    /// The DH multiplicative unit; renders `one`.
26	    NumberOne,
27	    /// The natural-number one; renders `%1`.
28	    NatOne,
29	    /// The DH group neutral element; renders `DH_neutral`.
30	    DhNeutral,
31	    /// Function application `f(a, b, …)`; arity 0 renders bare (`f`).
32	    App(String, Vec<Term>),
33	    /// Named binary algebra operator; `"exp"` renders infix `a^b`, anything
34	    /// else falls back to application form.
35	    AlgApp(String, Box<Term>, Box<Term>),
36	    /// Tuple `<a, b, c>`; a Pair in LAST position flattens into the enclosing
37	    /// tuple, a Pair in any other position keeps its own delimiters.
38	    Pair(Vec<Term>),
39	    /// Bi-system term `diff(l, r)`; renders in application form.
40	    Diff(Box<Term>, Box<Term>),
41	    BinOp(BinOp, Box<Term>, Box<Term>),
42	    /// Sapic pattern-match marker (`=t`). UNOBSERVABLE through the no-prove
43	    /// MSR echo — see BEHAVIOR.md; the rendering here is a flagged
44	    /// placeholder, not oracle-pinned.
45	    PatMatch(Box<Term>),
46	}
47	
48	/// Infix operators. Glyphs and parenthesization per BEHAVIOR.md: `Exp`
49	/// renders `a^b` with flat chains and no added parens; the four AC operators
50	/// render `(a<op>b<op>c)` — self-parenthesized, flattened, no spaces.
51	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
52	pub enum BinOp {
53	    Exp,     // ^
54	    Mult,    // *
55	    Union,   // ++
56	    Xor,     // ⊕ (U+2295)
57	    NatPlus, // %+
58	}
59	
60	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
61	pub struct VarSpec {
62	    pub name: String,
63	    /// Rendered as a `.idx` suffix when > 0 (`x.1`, `~x.7`).
64	    pub idx: u64,
65	    pub sort: SortHint,
66	    /// Source-level type annotation; not rendered in the theory echo.
67	    pub typ: Option<String>,
68	}
69	
70	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Default)]
71	pub enum SortHint {
72	    Msg,
73	    Pub,   // $x
74	    Fresh, // ~x
75	    Node,  // #i
76	    Nat,   // %n
77	    /// Source-suffix form (`x:pub`); the echo always shows the sigil form,
78	    /// so this renders identically to the corresponding sigil sort.
79	    Suffix(SuffixSort),
80	    #[default]
81	    Untagged,
82	}
83	
84	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
85	pub enum SuffixSort {
86	    Msg,
87	    Pub,
88	    Fresh,
89	    Node,
90	    Nat,
91	}
92	
93	// ── R1: signature block ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
94	
95	/// The DECLARED signature to render as `builtins:` / `functions:` /
96	/// `equations:`. The renderer itself performs the observable closure: builtin
97	/// expansion into function/equation entries, canonical builtin ordering,
98	/// dedup, and sorting (all oracle-pinned — BEHAVIOR.md "Signature section").
99	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Default)]
100	pub struct Signature {
101	    /// Builtin names as declared (`"diffie-hellman"`, `"hashing"`, …).
102	    pub builtins: Vec<String>,
103	    /// User-declared function symbols.
104	    pub functions: Vec<FunctionDecl>,
105	    /// User-declared equations.
106	    pub equations: Vec<Equation>,
107	    /// True when the user equation block carries `[convergent]`.
108	    pub convergent: bool,
109	}
110	
111	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]
112	pub struct FunctionDecl {
113	    pub name: String,
114	    pub arity: usize,
115	    pub private: bool,
116	    pub destructor: bool,
117	}
118	
119	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]
120	pub struct Equation {
121	    pub lhs: Term,
122	    pub rhs: Term,
123	}
124	
125	// ── R2: facts & rules ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
126	
127	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]
128	pub struct Fact {
129	    /// Renders a `!` prefix before the name.
130	    pub persistent: bool,
131	    pub name: String,
132	    pub args: Vec<Term>,
133	    /// Renders `[+, -, no_precomp]` attached after the closing paren, in
134	    /// that canonical order whatever the source order (probe:p_fann,
135	    /// target:seqdfsneeded).
136	    pub annotations: Vec<FactAnnotation>,
137	}
138	
139	/// Fact annotations as observed in the echo (probe:p_fann): `+` / `-` /
140	/// `no_precomp`.
141	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, PartialOrd, Ord)]
142	pub enum FactAnnotation {
143	    SolveFirst, // +
144	    SolveLast,  // -
145	    NoSources,  // no_precomp
146	}
147	
148	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]
149	pub struct Rule {
150	    pub name: String,
151	    /// `Some("E")` renders `rule (modulo E) name`; the AC rule inside a
152	    /// variants comment carries `Some("AC")`; `None` renders plain
153	    /// `rule name`.
154	    pub modulo: Option<String>,
155	    pub attributes: Vec<RuleAttr>,
156	    pub premises: Vec<Fact>,
157	    pub actions: Vec<Fact>,
158	    pub conclusions: Vec<Fact>,
159	    /// 0-based premise indices annotated `// loop breaker(s): [..]`
160	    /// (probes c_loop, p_lb2).
161	    pub loop_breakers: Vec<usize>,
162	}
163	
164	/// Rule attributes. Only color / no_derivcheck / issapicrule / role render,
165	/// in that canonical order (probe:p_rattr, target:issue713); `process=…` and
166	/// external attributes are dropped from the echo.
167	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]
168	pub enum RuleAttr {
169	    /// Renders `color=#<value>` (value as supplied, lowercased upstream).
170	    Color(String),
171	    NoDerivCheck,
172	    Role(String),
173	    IsSapicRule,
174	    Process(String),
175	    External(String, Option<String>),
176	}
177	
178	/// Pre-computed AC-variant data for a rule (from the ported solver); the
179	/// `variants (modulo AC)` comment renders from this. `None` at the render
180	/// call site means the trivial-variant comment.
181	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]
182	pub struct AcVariants {
183	    /// The rule normalized modulo AC, re-rendered inside the comment.
184	    pub ac_rule: Rule,
185	    /// Numbered substitution groups; each entry is (variable, term), both
186	    /// rendered by the R1 term core.
187	    pub substitutions: Vec<Vec<(Term, Term)>>,
188	}
189	
190	// ── R3: formulas, restrictions, lemmas ──────────────────────────────────────
191	
192	/// Trace formula. Rendered by `formula::render`; the statement printer
193	/// parenthesizes EVERY operand of a binary connective and every ¬ argument,
194	/// keeps quantifier bodies bare, and mirrors the source association of
195	/// chains (probe:q_p2 — left/right chains echo distinctly).
196	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]
197	pub enum Formula {
198	    /// Renders `⊥`.
199	    False,
200	    /// Renders `⊤`.
201	    True,
202	    Atom(Atom),
203	    /// Renders `¬(…)` — the argument always parenthesized (probe:q_p2 s8).
204	    Not(Box<Formula>),
205	    /// Renders `(l) ∧ (r)`.
206	    And(Box<Formula>, Box<Formula>),
207	    /// Renders `(l) ∨ (r)`.
208	    Or(Box<Formula>, Box<Formula>),
209	    /// Renders `(l) ⇒ (r)`.
210	    Implies(Box<Formula>, Box<Formula>),
211	    /// Renders `(l) ⇔ (r)` (probe:q_r2).
212	    Iff(Box<Formula>, Box<Formula>),
213	    /// Renders `∀ v1 v2. body` — binders in declaration order with their
214	    /// sort sigils (probe:q_b1), body bare at nest 1 (probe:q_l2).
215	    Forall(Vec<VarSpec>, Box<Formula>),
216	    /// Renders `∃ v1 v2. body`.
217	    Exists(Vec<VarSpec>, Box<Formula>),
218	}
219	
220	/// Formula atoms as observed in the echo (probes q_at1, q_l4, q_l5).
221	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]
222	pub enum Atom {
223	    /// `l = r`.
224	    Eq(Term, Term),
225	    /// `l < r` (temporal or nat ordering).
226	    Less(Term, Term),
227	    /// Multiset smaller. UNOBSERVABLE through the no-prove echo (no corpus
228	    /// witness, no reachable source spelling found) — rendered like `Less`
229	    /// as a flagged placeholder, see BEHAVIOR.md.
230	    LessMset(Term, Term),
231	    /// `l ⊏ r` (probe:q_at1, target:NumberSubtermTests).
232	    Subterm(Term, Term),
233	    /// `Fact( … ) @ tp` — `@ tp` attached beside the fact's last line
234	    /// (probe:q_l5).
235	    Action(Fact, Term),
236	    /// `last(tp)` — no interior spaces (probe:q_at1).
237	    Last(Term),
238	    /// Predicate fact atom. UNOBSERVABLE: predicates are expanded upstream
239	    /// of the echo (probe:q_pred1) — rendered as a bare fact, flagged.
240	    Pred(Fact),
241	}
242	
243	/// `restriction name: "formula"` plus the conditional `// safety formula`
244	/// line and the `/* expanded formula: … */` comment (probe:q_w1). The
245	/// `axiom` keyword echoes as a restriction too (probe:q_ax1).
246	///
247	/// `formula` is the STATEMENT as written (macros unexpanded); `expanded` is
248	/// the macro/predicate-expanded formula shown in the `expanded formula:`
249	/// comment. They differ ONLY when the restriction uses a macro
250	/// (target:MacroInLemmasAndRestrictions — statement `A( m(m3(x)) )`, expanded
251	/// `A( x )`); predicate expansion happens upstream of BOTH, so predicate-only
252	/// restrictions render the two identically (probe:q_pred1). `expanded` is a
253	/// caller-supplied opaque input (the ported macro/predicate expansion), NOT
254	/// derived here.
255	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]
256	pub struct Restriction {
257	    pub name: String,
258	    pub formula: Formula,
259	    pub expanded: Formula,
260	}
261	
262	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
263	pub enum TraceQuantifier {
264	    AllTraces,
265	    ExistsTrace,
266	}
267	
268	/// Lemma attributes as observed (probes q_la1, q_la2, q_la3, target:5G_AKA).
269	/// Rendered in SOURCE order, duplicates kept — no canonicalization
270	/// (probe:q_la1). Attributes without a corpus/probe witness (left/right,
271	/// output=…, diff-mode spellings) are NOT modeled — see the UNOBSERVABLE
272	/// register in BEHAVIOR.md.
273	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]
274	pub enum LemmaAttr {
275	    /// `sources`.
276	    Sources,
277	    /// `reuse`.
278	    Reuse,
279	    /// `use_induction`.
280	    UseInduction,
281	    /// `hide_lemma=<name>`.
282	    HideLemma(String),
283	    /// `heuristic=<value>` — the value string is carried verbatim, braces
284	    /// included (`S`, `{mytac}` — probes q_la2/q_la3).
285	    Heuristic(String),
286	}
287	
288	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]
289	pub struct Lemma {
290	    pub name: String,
291	    pub attributes: Vec<LemmaAttr>,
292	    pub trace_quantifier: TraceQuantifier,
293	    pub formula: Formula,
294	    /// Embedded proof script, re-rendered by the PORTED proof printer and
295	    /// handed here verbatim (column-0 multi-line text). `None` renders the
296	    /// no-prove placeholder `by sorry` (probe:q_w1, target:Yubikey).
297	    pub proof: Option<String>,
298	}
299	
300	/// Pre-computed guarded-formula comment body (from the ported guarded
301	/// transform), rendered inside `/* … */` after the lemma statement. The
302	/// header line is chosen by the renderer from the lemma's trace quantifier;
303	/// the CONTENT is opaque input (probe:q_w1, probe:q_r1).
304	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]
305	pub enum Guarded {
306	    /// Successful conversion: the quoted multi-line guarded-formula block
307	    /// (quotes included), emitted verbatim at column 0.
308	    Formula(String),
309	    /// Failed conversion: the raw error text (column-0 lines, as the
310	    /// transform reports it); the comment frame indents every line by 2
311	    /// under a `conversion to guarded formula failed:` header (probe:q_r1).
312	    Failed(String),
313	}
314	
315	// ── R4: macros / predicates ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
316	
317	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]
318	pub struct Macro {
319	    pub name: String,
320	    pub params: Vec<Term>,
321	    pub body: Term,
322	}
323	
324	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]
325	pub struct Predicate {
326	    pub name: String,
327	    pub params: Vec<VarSpec>,
328	    pub body: Formula,
329	}
330	
331	// ── top-level theory ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
332	
333	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Default)]
334	pub struct Theory {
335	    pub name: String,
336	    pub items: Vec<TheoryItem>,
337	}
338	
339	/// One top-level theory item, in the order it appears in the echo (source
340	/// order; the signature is NOT an item — it is rendered first from the
341	/// separate `Signature`). The frame (`theory::render`) stacks these with a
342	/// blank line between successive items. Rules carry their pre-computed
343	/// `AcVariants` and lemmas their pre-computed `Guarded` (solver inputs) so the
344	/// frame can dispatch to the R2/R3 entry points with the right arguments.
345	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]
346	pub enum TheoryItem {
347	    /// The `macros:` block (rendered as one item).
348	    Macros(Vec<Macro>),
349	    /// A contiguous run of `predicate:` declarations (rendered as one item,
350	    /// blank-line separated internally).
351	    Predicates(Vec<Predicate>),
352	    /// One `rule (modulo E) …` block plus its AC-variants comment.
353	    Rule(Rule, Option<AcVariants>),
354	    /// One `restriction …` block (also legacy `axiom`).
355	    Restriction(Restriction),
356	    /// One `lemma …` block plus its guarded-formula comment.
357	    Lemma(Lemma, Option<Guarded>),
358	    /// A theory-level `heuristic: <value>` line.
359	    Heuristic(String),
360	    /// An opaque top-level block carried through the frame VERBATIM (the
361	    /// whole contiguous `tactic:` region, `section{* … *}` / `text{* … *}`
362	    /// formal comments, `options`, user `/* … */` comments, …). Like the
363	    /// guarded and proof text, its interior layout is outside this crate's
364	    /// erasure surface — it is a pre-rendered input, re-emitted unchanged.
365	    Verbatim(String),
366	}
367

input.command:
mkdir -p /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/pretty/workspace/scratchpad/r6/raw
python3 - <<'PY'
import json, glob, os, re
CORPUS="/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/weblayer/producers/oracle/captured_responses"
OUT="/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/pretty/workspace/scratchpad/r6/raw"
IDX=re.compile(r"/thy/(trace|equiv)/[^/]+/")
def label(p, m):
    h=os.path.basename(p).split(".")[0][:8]
    name=None
    for url,r in m.items():
        if r.get("kind")=="html" and "/overview/" in url:
            mm=re.search(r"<title>Theory:\s*([^<]*)</title>", r.get("body",""))
            if mm: name=mm.group(1).strip(); break
    if name is None:
        for url,r in m.items():
            if r.get("kind")=="text" and url.endswith("/source"):
                mm=re.match(r"theory\s+(\S+)", r.get("body",""))
                if mm: name=mm.group(1); break
    name=re.sub(r"[^A-Za-z0-9_.-]","_", name or "UNKNOWN")
    return f"{name}__{h}"
n=0
for p in sorted(glob.glob(CORPUS+"/*.hs.json")):
    d=json.load(open(p)); m=d["manifest"]; lbl=label(p,m)
    for route,fam in (("/thy/trace/#/main/message","message"),("/thy/trace/#/main/rules","rules")):
        if route in m:
            body=m[route]["body"]  # raw envelope string
            with open(os.path.join(OUT, f"{lbl}__{fam}.raw"),"w") as f:
                f.write(body)
            n+=1
print("wrote",n,"raw envelopes to",OUT)
PY

input.command: cd /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/graphdot/sanctioned/pretty-1.1.3.6/src/Text/PrettyPrint/Annotated && awk '/^best /,/^fits /' HughesPJ.hs | head -80

content.content:
best :: Int   -- Line length.
     -> Int   -- Ribbon length.
     -> RDoc a
     -> RDoc a  -- No unions in here!.
best w0 r = get w0
  where
    get w _ | w == 0 && False = undefined
    get _ Empty               = Empty
    get _ NoDoc               = NoDoc
    get w (NilAbove p)        = nilAbove_ (get w p)
    get w (TextBeside s p)    = textBeside_ s (get1 w (annotSize s) p)
    get w (Nest k p)          = nest_ k (get (w - k) p)
    get w (p `Union` q)       = nicest w r (get w p) (get w q)
    get _ (Above {})          = error "best get Above"
    get _ (Beside {})         = error "best get Beside"

    get1 w _ _ | w == 0 && False  = undefined
    get1 _ _  Empty               = Empty
    get1 _ _  NoDoc               = NoDoc
    get1 w sl (NilAbove p)        = nilAbove_ (get (w - sl) p)
    get1 w sl (TextBeside s p)    = textBeside_ s (get1 w (sl + annotSize s) p)
    get1 w sl (Nest _ p)          = get1 w sl p
    get1 w sl (p `Union` q)       = nicest1 w r sl (get1 w sl p)
                                                   (get1 w sl q)
    get1 _ _  (Above {})          = error "best get1 Above"
    get1 _ _  (Beside {})         = error "best get1 Beside"

nicest :: Int -> Int -> Doc a -> Doc a -> Doc a
nicest !w !r = nicest1 w r 0

nicest1 :: Int -> Int -> Int -> Doc a -> Doc a -> Doc a
nicest1 !w !r !sl p q | fits ((w `min` r) - sl) p = p
                      | otherwise                 = q

fits :: Int  -- Space available

content.text: Let me read the sanctioned `sep1`/`sepNB`/`fits` and `fill` to check for a port discrepancy in nest handling.

input.command: cd /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/graphdot/sanctioned/pretty-1.1.3.6/src/Text/PrettyPrint/Annotated && sed -n '/^sepX/,/^oneLiner/p' HughesPJ.hs | head -90

content.content:
sepX :: Bool -> [Doc a] -> Doc a
sepX _ []     = empty
sepX x (p:ps) = sep1 x (reduceDoc p) 0 ps


-- Specification: sep1 g k ys = sep (x : map (nest k) ys)
--                            = oneLiner (x <g> nest k (hsep ys))
--                              `union` x $$ nest k (vcat ys)
sep1 :: Bool -> RDoc a -> Int -> [Doc a] -> RDoc a
sep1 _ _                   k _  | k `seq` False = undefined
sep1 _ NoDoc               _ _  = NoDoc
sep1 g (p `Union` q)       k ys = sep1 g p k ys `union_`
                                  aboveNest q False k (reduceDoc (vcat ys))

sep1 g Empty               k ys = mkNest k (sepX g ys)
sep1 g (Nest n p)          k ys = nest_ n (sep1 g p (k - n) ys)

sep1 _ (NilAbove p)        k ys = nilAbove_
                                  (aboveNest p False k (reduceDoc (vcat ys)))
sep1 g (TextBeside s p) k ys    = textBeside_ s (sepNB g p (k - annotSize s) ys)
sep1 _ (Above {})          _ _  = error "sep1 Above"
sep1 _ (Beside {})         _ _  = error "sep1 Beside"

-- Specification: sepNB p k ys = sep1 (text "" <> p) k ys
-- Called when we have already found some text in the first item
-- We have to eat up nests
sepNB :: Bool -> Doc a -> Int -> [Doc a] -> Doc a
sepNB g (Nest _ p) k ys
  = sepNB g p k ys -- Never triggered, because of invariant (2)
sepNB g Empty k ys
  = oneLiner (nilBeside g (reduceDoc rest)) `mkUnion`
    -- XXX: TODO: PRETTY: Used to use True here (but GHC used False...)
    nilAboveNest False k (reduceDoc (vcat ys))
  where
    rest | g         = hsep ys
         | otherwise = hcat ys
sepNB g p k ys
  = sep1 g p k ys


-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- @fill@

-- | \"Paragraph fill\" version of 'cat'.
fcat :: [Doc a] -> Doc a
fcat = fill False

-- | \"Paragraph fill\" version of 'sep'.
fsep :: [Doc a] -> Doc a
fsep = fill True

-- Specification:
--
-- fill g docs = fillIndent 0 docs
--
-- fillIndent k [] = []
-- fillIndent k [p] = p
-- fillIndent k (p1:p2:ps) =
--    oneLiner p1 <g> fillIndent (k + length p1 + g ? 1 : 0)
--                               (remove_nests (oneLiner p2) : ps)
--     `Union`
--    (p1 $*$ nest (-k) (fillIndent 0 ps))
--
-- $*$ is defined for layouts (not Docs) as
-- layout1 $*$ layout2 | hasMoreThanOneLine layout1 = layout1 $$ layout2
--                     | otherwise                  = layout1 $+$ layout2

fill :: Bool -> [Doc a] -> RDoc a
fill _ []     = empty
fill g (p:ps) = fill1 g (reduceDoc p) 0 ps

fill1 :: Bool -> RDoc a -> Int -> [Doc a] -> Doc a
fill1 _ _                   k _  | k `seq` False = undefined
fill1 _ NoDoc               _ _  = NoDoc
fill1 g (p `Union` q)       k ys = fill1 g p k ys `union_`
                                   aboveNest q False k (fill g ys)
fill1 g Empty               k ys = mkNest k (fill g ys)
fill1 g (Nest n p)          k ys = nest_ n (fill1 g p (k - n) ys)
fill1 g (NilAbove p)        k ys = nilAbove_ (aboveNest p False k (fill g ys))
fill1 g (TextBeside s p)    k ys = textBeside_ s (fillNB g p (k - annotSize s) ys)
fill1 _ (Above {})          _ _  = error "fill1 Above"
fill1 _ (Beside {})         _ _  = error "fill1 Beside"

fillNB :: Bool -> Doc a -> Int -> [Doc a] -> Doc a
fillNB _ _           k _  | k `seq` False = undefined
fillNB g (Nest _ p)  k ys   = fillNB g p k ys
                              -- Never triggered, because of invariant (2)
fillNB _ Empty _ []         = Empty
fillNB g Empty k (Empty:ys) = fillNB g Empty k ys
fillNB g Empty k (y:ys)     = fillNBE g k y ys

content.content:
nilAboveNest :: Bool -> Int -> RDoc a -> RDoc a
nilAboveNest _ k _           | k `seq` False = undefined
nilAboveNest _ _ Empty       = Empty
                               -- Here's why the "text s <>" is in the spec!
nilAboveNest g k (Nest k1 q) = nilAboveNest g (k + k1) q
nilAboveNest g k q           | not g && k > 0      -- No newline if no overlap
                             = textBeside_ (NoAnnot (Str (indent k)) k) q
                             | otherwise           -- Put them really above
                             = nilAbove_ (mkNest k q)

=== fillNBE ===
fillNBE :: Bool -> Int -> Doc a -> [Doc a] -> Doc a
fillNBE g k y ys
  = nilBeside g (fill1 g ((elideNest . oneLiner . reduceDoc) y) k' ys)
    -- XXX: TODO: PRETTY: Used to use True here (but GHC used False...)
    `mkUnion` nilAboveNest False k (fill g (y:ys))
  where k' = if g then k - 1 else k

=== elideNest / spaces / indent ===
377:indent :: Int -> String
indent :: Int -> String
indent !n = replicate n ' '
